377: - **Tauri Runtime**: Desktop shell and IPC infrastructure
378: 
379: **External Systems:**
380: - **OS Process Manager**: Child process lifecycle management
381: - **File System**: Workspace I/O operations
382: - **LLM APIs**: Via core domain delegation
383: 
384: ---
385: 
386: **Document Maintenance:**  
387: This documentation should be updated when:
388: - New Tauri commands are added to the public API
389: - Event payload structures change
390: - Cross-platform process handling logic is modified
391: - State management patterns evolve
392: 
393: **Contact:**  
394: For technical questions regarding the GUI Backend Domain, refer to the `cowork-gui/src-tauri/src/` directory structure and associated Rust module documentation.
````

### litho.docs/en/4.Deep-Exploration/GUI Frontend Domain.md (649 lines)

````
1: **GUI Frontend Domain Technical Documentation**
2: 
3: **Generation Time:** 2026-02-14 05:21:52 (UTC)  
4: **Version:** 1.0  
5: **Domain:** Presentation Layer (Cowork-GUI)  
6: 
7: ---
8: 
9: ## 1. Executive Summary
10: 
11: The **GUI Frontend Domain** implements the desktop presentation layer of the Cowork Forge system, delivering a comprehensive AI-assisted development environment through a Tauri-based desktop application shell with a React 18 frontend. This domain provides the primary human-computer interface for the 7-stage AI agent pipeline, enabling real-time iteration monitoring, artifact inspection, code editing, and Human-in-the-Loop (HITL) validation workflows.
12: 
13: **Key Architectural Characteristics:**
14: - **Multi-Panel Workspace**: Eight functional panels (Projects, Iterations, Chat/Artifacts, Code Editor, Runner, Preview, Memory, Knowledge) orchestrated through a central state manager
15: - **Event-Driven Real-Time Communication**: Bidirectional IPC via Tauri's invoke/listen APIs supporting streaming AI agent outputs and asynchronous HITL interactions
16: - **Dual-Mode Interaction**: Supports both automated execution monitoring and interactive feedback loops with modal-based confirmation workflows
17: - **Performance-Optimized Rendering**: Virtualized lists for large file trees, Monaco Editor integration for code editing, and optimistic UI updates for responsive feedback
18: 
19: ---
20: 
21: ## 2. System Architecture
22: 
23: ### 2.1 Container Context (C4 Level 2)
24: 
25: The GUI Frontend operates as a distinct container within the Cowork Forge architecture, communicating exclusively with the Tauri Backend Domain through well-defined IPC boundaries:
26: 
27: ```mermaid
28: flowchart TB
29:     subgraph GUIContainer["cowork-gui (Tauri Application)"]
30:         React["React 18 Frontend<br/>(Ant Design + Monaco)"]
31:         TauriCmd["Tauri Commands<br/>(Rust Bridge)"]
32:         EventBus["Event Listener Hub<br/>(13+ Event Types)"]
33:     end
34:     
35:     subgraph CoreContainer["cowork-core (Domain Engine)"]
36:         Pipeline["Pipeline Controller"]
37:         Domain["Domain Logic"]
38:         Tools["Tool Ecosystem"]
39:     end
40:     
41:     subgraph External["External Systems"]
42:         FileSystem["Local File System"]
43:         LLM["LLM APIs"]
44:     end
45:     
46:     React <-->|invoke/listen| TauriCmd
47:     TauriCmd -->|Implements| Interaction["InteractiveBackend Trait"]
48:     Interaction -->|Drives| Pipeline
49:     Pipeline -->|Uses| Tools
50:     Tools -->|Access| FileSystem
51:     Pipeline -->|Calls| LLM
52:     
53:     TauriCmd -->|Emits| EventBus
54:     EventBus -->|Updates| React
55: ```
56: 
57: ### 2.2 Component Architecture (C4 Level 3)
58: 
59: The frontend implements a layered component hierarchy with clear separation of concerns:
60: 
61: ```mermaid
62: flowchart TB
63:     subgraph EntryLayer["Entry Layer"]
64:         Main[main.tsx<br/>Theme & React Root]
65:         IndexCSS[Index Styling<br/>Ant Design Theme]
66:     end
67:     
68:     subgraph OrchestrationLayer["Core Orchestration"]
69:         App[App.tsx<br/>State Management Hub + Zustand Stores]
70:         EventManager[Event Listener Manager<br/>agent_event, tool_call, input_request]
71:         InputModal[HITL Input Modal<br/>Confirmation & Feedback]
72:     end
73:     
74:     subgraph PanelLayer["Panel Registry"]
75:         Projects[ProjectsPanel<br/>Project Management]
76:         Iterations[IterationsPanel<br/>7-Stage Lifecycle]
77:         Chat[ChatPanel<br/>Pipeline & PM Agent Chat]
78:         Artifacts[ArtifactsViewer<br/>Markdown Viewer]
79:         Editor[CodeEditor<br/>Monaco + Virtual Tree]
80:         Runner[RunnerPanel<br/>Process Logs]
81:         Preview[PreviewPanel<br/>Dev Server IFrame]
82:         Memory[MemoryPanel<br/>Query & Browse]
83:         Knowledge[KnowledgePanel<br/>Knowledge Repository]
84:         Settings[SettingsPanel<br/>Configuration]
85:     end
86:     
87:     subgraph Communication["IPC Layer"]
88:         Commands[Command Invokers<br/>gui_*, query_*]
89:         Listeners[Event Listeners<br/>13+ Event Types]
90:         Types[TypeScript Types<br/>Serde-mapped DTOs]
91:     end
92:     
93:     Main --> App
94:     App --> EventManager
95:     App --> InputModal
96:     App --> Projects
97:     App --> Iterations
98:     App --> Chat
99:     App --> Editor
100:     App --> Runner
101:     App --> Preview
102:     App --> Memory
103:     App --> Knowledge
104:     
105:     Projects --> Commands
106:     Iterations --> Commands
107:     Editor --> Commands
108:     Memory --> Commands
109:     
110:     EventManager --> Listeners
111:     Listeners --> Chat
112:     Listeners --> Iterations
113:     Listeners --> Runner
114: ```
115: 
116: ---
117: 
118: ## 3. Technical Implementation
119: 
120: ### 3.1 Technology Stack
121: 
122: | Layer | Technology | Purpose |
123: |-------|-----------|---------|
124: | **UI Framework** | React 18 + TypeScript | Component-based declarative UI with type safety |
125: | **Component Library** | Ant Design 5.x | Enterprise-grade UI components (Tables, Modals, Tabs) |
126: | **Desktop Shell** | Tauri 1.x+ | Rust-based desktop runtime with native OS integration |
127: | **Code Editor** | Monaco Editor | VS Code-powered editing with syntax highlighting and IntelliSense |
128: | **Virtualization** | react-window | Performance optimization for large file trees and lists |
129: | **Content Rendering** | ReactMarkdown + remark-gfm | Markdown parsing for AI-generated artifacts |
130: | **Styling** | CSS Modules + Ant Design Tokens | Scoped component styles with theme consistency |
131: | **State Management** | Zustand | Lightweight state management with stores (project, agent, UI) |
132: | **Icons** | @ant-design/icons | Consistent iconography across interface |
133: 
134: ### 3.2 State Management Architecture
135: 
136: The domain employs a **Zustand-based centralized state management** pattern with three dedicated stores:
137: 
138: **Store Architecture:**
139: - **Project Store** (`useProjectStore`): Project and iteration state, current session context
140: - **Agent Store** (`useAgentStore`): Chat messages, PM messages, processing state, input requests
141: - **UI Store** (`useUIStore`): Active view, refresh triggers, command palette state
142: 
143: **Central State Hub (App.tsx):**
144: - `currentSession`: Active project/iteration context
145: - `activeView`: Current visible panel (projects, iterations, chat, code, run, memory, knowledge, settings)
146: - `messages[]`: Real-time AI agent message buffer with type differentiation (user, agent, thinking, tool_call, tool_result)
147: - `pmMessages[]`: Project Manager Agent chat message buffer (user, pm_agent)
148: - `chatMode`: Chat mode identifier (pipeline, pm_agent, disabled), automatically switches based on iteration status
149: - `isProcessing`: Global execution state for pipeline operations
150: - `inputRequest`: HITL modal state management
151: - `refreshTriggers`: Counter-based cache invalidation for memory/knowledge panels
152: 
153: **State Distribution Pattern:**
154: ```javascript
155: // Centralized state with selective prop drilling
156: <App>
157:   <ProjectsPanel currentSession={session} onProjectSelect={handler} />
158:   <IterationsPanel 
159:     currentSession={session} 
160:     isProcessing={isProcessing}
161:     onExecuteStatusChange={handler}
162:     refreshTrigger={refreshTrigger}
163:   />
164:   <CodeEditor currentIterationId={iterationId} />
165:   {/* ... additional panels */}
166: </App>
167: ```
168: 
169: **Event-Driven State Updates:**
170: Rather than polling, the system maintains WebSocket-like connectivity through Tauri's event system:
171: - **Emission**: Backend emits events via `AppHandle` in Rust
172: - **Subscription**: Frontend registers listeners in `useEffect` hooks with cleanup
173: - **Aggregation**: Streaming content aggregated in local state buffers
174: 
175: ---
176: 
177: ## 4. Communication Layer (IPC)
178: 
179: ### 4.1 Command Interface (Frontend → Backend)
180: 
181: The domain exposes type-safe command invocations using Tauri's `invoke` API:
182: 
183: **Project Management Commands:**
184: | Command | Parameters | Return | Description |
185: |---------|-----------|--------|-------------|
186: | `gui_init_project` | `{ name, description?, path? }` | `ProjectDTO` | Initialize new project workspace |
187: | `get_all_projects` | - | `ProjectDTO[]` | Retrieve registered projects |
188: | `open_project` | `{ projectId, newWindow? }` | `void` | Load project in current/new window |
189: | `delete_project` | `{ projectId }` | `boolean` | Remove project from registry |
190: | `update_project` | `{ projectId, updates }` | `ProjectDTO` | Modify project metadata |
191: 
192: **Iteration Control Commands:**
193: | Command | Parameters | Return | Description |
194: |---------|-----------|--------|-------------|
195: | `gui_create_iteration` | `{ projectId, title, description, fromIteration? }` | `IterationDTO` | Create genesis/evolution iteration |
196: | `gui_execute_iteration` | `{ iterationId }` | `void` | Start pipeline execution (async) |
197: | `gui_continue_iteration` | `{ iterationId }` | `void` | Resume paused iteration |
198: | `gui_retry_iteration` | `{ iterationId, stage? }` | `void` | Retry failed stage |
199: | `gui_delete_iteration` | `{ iterationId }` | `boolean` | Remove iteration workspace |
200: 
201: **File Operations:**
202: | Command | Parameters | Return | Description |
203: |---------|-----------|--------|-------------|
204: | `get_iteration_file_tree` | `{ iterationId }` | `FileNode[]` | Retrieve virtualized file tree |
205: | `read_iteration_file` | `{ iterationId, filePath }` | `string` | Read file content |
206: | `save_iteration_file` | `{ iterationId, filePath, content }` | `boolean` | Persist file changes |
207: | `format_code` | `{ code, language }` | `string` | Format code using external formatter |
208: 
209: **Knowledge & Memory:**
210: | Command | Parameters | Return | Description |
211: |---------|-----------|--------|-------------|
212: | `query_memory_index` | `{ scope, category?, stage?, limit?, keyword? }` | `MemoryEntry[]` | Search project memory |
213: | `load_memory_detail` | `{ memoryId }` | `MemoryDetail` | Retrieve full memory content |
214: | `gui_get_project_knowledge` | `{ projectId }` | `KnowledgeDTO` | Get aggregated knowledge |
215: | `gui_regenerate_knowledge` | `{ iterationId }` | `void` | Trigger knowledge re-extraction |
216: 
217: ### 4.2 Event Interface (Backend → Frontend)
218: 
219: The system implements 13+ event types for real-time streaming:
220: 
221: **Lifecycle Events:**
222: - `project_loaded`: Emitted when project context initialized
223: - `iteration_started`: Pipeline execution commenced
224: - `iteration_completed`: Successful completion with results
225: - `iteration_failed`: Error state with diagnostic messages
226: - `iteration_continued`: Resumed from paused state
227: 
228: **Agent Streaming Events:**
229: - `agent_event`: Structured content messages (thinking, progress, stage transitions)
230: - `agent_streaming`: Real-time token streaming from LLM APIs
231: 
232: **Execution Events:**
233: - `tool_call`: Tool invocation with name and arguments
234: - `tool_result`: Tool execution result (success/failure with output)
235: - `progress`: Stage completion percentage and status
236: 
237: **HITL Events:**
238: - `input_request`: Suspension for human input with metadata (type, options, context)
239: - `input_response`: (Command) Response submission from frontend
240: 
241: **Process Events:**
242: - `project_log`: Stdout/stderr from development server processes
243: - `project_error`: Process execution errors
244: 
245: **Implementation Pattern:**
246: ```javascript
247: // Event subscription in App.tsx
248: useEffect(() => {
249:   const unlisten = listen('agent_event', (event) => {
250:     const { payload } = event;
251:     setMessages(prev => [...prev, {
252:       type: payload.type, // 'thinking' | 'message' | 'stage'
253:       content: payload.content,
254:       timestamp: Date.now()
255:     }]);
256:   });
257:   
258:   return () => { unlisten.then(f => f()); };
259: }, []);
260: ```
261: 
262: ---
263: 
264: ## 5. Functional Components
265: 
266: ### 5.1 Projects Panel (`ProjectsPanel.tsx`)
267: 
268: **Responsibilities:**
269: - Project lifecycle management (CRUD operations)
270: - Multi-window project loading support
271: - Workspace directory access integration
272: - Technology stack visualization
273: 
274: **Key Features:**
275: - **Card-based Layout**: Grid display of projects with metadata (tech stack, last modified, iteration count)
276: - **Multi-window Support**: "Open in New Window" functionality using Tauri's window management API
277: - **Quick Actions**: Direct access to workspace directories via OS file explorer
278: - **Deletion Safety**: Confirmation modals with warning states for destructive operations
279: 
280: **State Interactions:**
281: - Emits `project_loaded` event on selection
282: - Triggers `activeView` transition to 'iterations'
283: - Manages `currentSession` context for child components
284: 
285: ### 5.2 Iterations Panel (`IterationsPanel.tsx`)
286: 
287: **Responsibilities:**
288: - 7-stage pipeline visualization (Idea → PRD → Design → Plan → Coding → Check → Delivery)
289: - Iteration execution control (Start, Pause, Continue, Retry)
290: - Real-time status tracking and progress indication
291: - Evolution iteration creation with inheritance mode selection
292: 
293: **Key Features:**
294: - **Stage Visualization**: Visual pipeline showing current stage, completed stages, and pending stages with color-coded status indicators
295: - **Execution Controls**: Context-aware buttons (Start for Draft, Continue for Paused, Retry for Failed)
296: - **Evolution Workflow**: "Evolve" button triggering inheritance mode selection (Full/Partial/None)
297: - **Live Status**: Real-time updates via `iteration_started`, `iteration_completed` events
298: 
299: **HITL Integration:**
300: - Listens for `input_request` events with `ARTIFACT_TYPE` context
301: - Auto-switches view to appropriate panel (Artifacts/Code) based on confirmation type
302: - Manages execution blocking during human validation phases
303: 
304: ### 5.3 Code Editor (`CodeEditor.tsx`)
305: 
306: **Responsibilities:**
307: - File tree navigation with virtualized rendering
308: - Multi-tab code editing with Monaco Editor
309: - File content persistence
310: - Language-specific syntax highlighting and formatting
311: 
312: **Technical Implementation:**
313: - **Virtualized Tree**: Uses `react-window` for performant rendering of large directory structures (>1000 files)
314: - **Monaco Integration**: Full-featured editor with IntelliSense, error highlighting, and minimap
315: - **Language Detection**: Automatic mode setting based on file extensions (rs, js, ts, py, html, css, json, md, toml, yaml)
316: - **Auto-save**: Debounced save operations to backend via `save_iteration_file`
317: 
318: **File Operations:**
319: - Lazy loading: File content fetched on-demand via `read_iteration_file`
320: - Change tracking: Dirty state management for unsaved changes
321: - Cross-panel integration: File selection from Chat/Artifacts panel opens in editor
322: 
323: ### 5.4 Runner & Preview Panels (`RunnerPanel.tsx`, `PreviewPanel.tsx`)
324: 
325: **Responsibilities:**
326: - Development server process management
327: - Real-time log streaming with ANSI sequence processing
328: - Live preview via iframe embedding
329: - Process lifecycle control (start, stop, restart)
330: 
331: **Technical Features:**
332: - **Log Streaming**: Consumes `project_log` events with ANSI color code rendering for terminal-like output
333: - **Log Filtering**: Search and filter capabilities for process output
334: - **Dual-Tab Interface**: "Run Program" (logs) and "Page Preview" (iframe) modes
335: - **Safety Controls**: Graceful process termination and port conflict detection
336: 
337: ### 5.5 Memory & Knowledge Panels (`MemoryPanel.tsx`, `KnowledgePanel.tsx`)
338: 
339: **Responsibilities:**
340: - Project memory querying and browsing
341: - Knowledge artifact visualization
342: - Historical decision and pattern retrieval
343: - Fuzzy search across memory entries
344: 
345: **Key Features:**
346: - **Query Interface**: Filter by scope (project/iteration), category (decision/pattern/insight), and keywords
347: - **Markdown Rendering**: Rich content display with syntax highlighting for code blocks
348: - **Detail Modals**: Drill-down views for memory entries with full context
349: - **Refresh Coordination**: Responds to `knowledge_regeneration_completed` events
350: 
351: ---
352: 
353: ## 6. Human-in-the-Loop (HITL) Implementation
354: 
355: The GUI Frontend implements sophisticated HITL workflows for AI-human collaboration:
356: 
357: ### 6.1 Confirmation Flow Architecture
358: 
359: ```mermaid
360: sequenceDiagram
361:     participant Agent as AI Agent
362:     participant Backend as Tauri Backend
363:     participant Frontend as React Frontend
364:     participant User as Developer
365:     
366:     Agent->>Backend: Request confirmation
367:     Backend->>Frontend: emit input_request event
368:     Frontend->>Frontend: Update state with request
369:     Frontend->>Frontend: Switch to relevant view
370:     Frontend->>User: Display modal with content preview
371:     
372:     alt User selects Pass
373:         User->>Frontend: Click Pass button
374:         Frontend->>Backend: Submit response with pass choice
375:         Backend->>Agent: Resume execution
376:     else User selects Edit
377:         User->>Frontend: Click Edit button
378:         Frontend->>Backend: Open external editor
379:         Backend->>Frontend: Wait for file change
380:         Frontend->>Backend: Detect changes or timeout
381:         Backend->>Agent: Resume with user edits
382:     else User selects Feedback
383:         User->>Frontend: Click Feedback button
384:         Frontend->>Frontend: Show feedback input area
385:         User->>Frontend: Enter feedback text
386:         Frontend->>Backend: Submit response with feedback and text
387:         Backend->>Agent: Execute with feedback
388:         Agent->>Agent: Regenerate content
389:         Agent->>Backend: New confirmation request
390:         Backend->>Frontend: New input request event
391:     end
392: ```
393: 
394: ### 6.2 Modal State Management
395: 
396: The `InputModal` component handles three interaction modes:
397: 1. **Text Input**: Freeform feedback for regeneration requests
398: 2. **Option Selection**: Predefined choices (Yes/No/View/Feedback) for artifact confirmation
399: 3. **Artifact Review**: Rich content display with tab switching to relevant panels
400: 
401: **Timeout Handling:**
402: - Backend maintains 3000-second timeout (50 minutes) for HITL responses
403: - Frontend displays countdown warnings for long-running operations
404: - Automatic cleanup on component unmount to prevent memory leaks
405: 
406: ---
407: 
408: ## 7. Performance Optimizations
409: 
410: ### 7.1 Virtualization Strategy
411: 
412: **File Tree Rendering:**
413: - Implements `react-window` FixedSizeTree for directories with >100 items
414: - Lazy loading of file metadata (size, modification time)
415: - Collapsed node optimization (children not rendered until expanded)
416: 
417: **Memory Lists:**
418: - Virtualized scrolling for memory entries with dynamic height support
419: - Pagination via "Load More" pattern for large result sets
420: - Debounced search input (300ms) to prevent excessive query commands
421: 
422: ### 7.2 Streaming Optimization
423: 
424: **Message Aggregation:**
425: - Buffers rapid `agent_streaming` events (throttled to 60fps updates)
426: - Uses `requestAnimationFrame` for smooth scrolling during high-velocity token streaming
427: - Implements message deduplication for tool_call/tool_result pairs
428: 
429: **Memoization:**
430: - `React.memo` on panel components to prevent unnecessary re-renders during background streaming
431: - `useMemo` for expensive markdown parsing and syntax highlighting
432: - `useCallback` for event handlers passed to child components
433: 
434: ---
435: 
436: ## 8. Integration Patterns
437: 
438: ### 8.1 Error Handling Strategy
439: 
440: **Command Errors:**
441: - Rust `Result` types mapped to JavaScript exceptions
442: - Ant Design `message` API for transient error notifications
443: - Modal dialogs for critical failures (workspace access denied, iteration corruption)
444: 
445: **Event Stream Errors:**
446: - `iteration_failed` events trigger error boundary catches
447: - Automatic state reset to prevent UI desynchronization
448: - Retry mechanisms with exponential backoff for transient network issues
449: 
450: ### 8.2 Cross-Panel Communication
451: 
452: **Refresh Coordination:**
453: ```javascript
454: // Counter-based invalidation pattern
455: const [memoryRefreshTrigger, setMemoryRefreshTrigger] = useState(0);
456: 
457: // Trigger from App.tsx when iteration completes
458: useEffect(() => {
459:   if (iterationCompleted) {
460:     setMemoryRefreshTrigger(prev => prev + 1);
461:   }
462: }, [iterationCompleted]);
463: 
464: // Consumption in MemoryPanel
465: useEffect(() => {
466:   loadMemoryData();
467: }, [memoryRefreshTrigger]);
468: ```
469: 
470: **View Switching:**
471: - Programmatic navigation via `setActiveView` prop drilling
472: - Context-aware switching (e.g., artifact confirmation switches to Code view for code files, Artifacts view for markdown)
473: 
474: ---
475: 
476: ## 9. Security Considerations
477: 
478: **Path Validation:**
479: - All file paths validated by backend `runtime_security` module before operations
480: - Frontend displays workspace-relative paths only (absolute paths obscured)
481: - Traversal attack prevention via backend path canonicalization
482: 
483: **Content Security:**
484: - IFrame sandboxing for Preview panel (`sandbox="allow-scripts allow-same-origin"`)
485: - Monaco Editor runs in isolated context
486: - Markdown rendering sanitizes HTML to prevent XSS via AI-generated content
487: 
488: **Process Isolation:**
489: - Development servers spawned in separate processes with restricted environment
490: - Frontend cannot execute arbitrary shell commands (all commands validated by backend Tool domain)
491: 
492: ---
493: 
494: ## 10. Known Limitations & Technical Debt
495: 
496: **Current Issues:**
497: 1. **Large Component**: `App.tsx` exceeds 800 LOC with complex state management; candidate for component splitting
498: 2. **Memory Leaks**: Event listeners in early returns may not cleanup properly if component unmounts during async operations
499: 3. **Chat History Performance**: Long-running iterations may accumulate large message arrays without virtualization
500: 
501: **Future Improvements:**
502: - Component splitting: Extract chat logic, iteration management, and panel rendering into separate modules
503: - Optimize message handling for very long-running iterations
504: - Consider WebSocket fallback for Tauri IPC in potential web deployment scenarios
505: 
506: ---
507: 
508: ## 11. Development Guidelines
509: 
510: **Adding New Panels:**
511: 1. Create component in `src/components/`
512: 2. Add panel key to `activeView` state in UI store (`src/stores/uiStore.ts`)
513: 3. Add menu item in `App.tsx` Sider menu with icon and label
514: 4. Add rendering logic in `App.tsx` renderContent function
515: 5. Add necessary IPC commands to Tauri backend
516: 6. Register event listeners in panel's `useEffect`
517: 
518: **Event Listener Best Practices:**
519: ```javascript
520: useEffect(() => {
521:   let unlisten;
522:   
523:   const setupListener = async () => {
524:     unlisten = await listen('event_name', handler);
525:   };
526:   
527:   setupListener();
528:   
529:   return () => {
530:     if (unlisten) unlisten.then(f => f());
531:   };
532: }, []);
533: ```
534: 
535: **Styling Conventions:**
536: - Use Ant Design tokens for consistency (`token.colorPrimary`, `token.borderRadius`)
537: - CSS Modules for component-scoped styles
538: - Responsive breakpoints: `xs` (<576px), `sm` (≥576px), `md` (≥768px), `lg` (≥992px), `xl` (≥1200px)
539: 
540: ---
541: 
542: **Conclusion:** The GUI Frontend Domain successfully abstracts the complexity of the AI agent pipeline into an intuitive desktop interface. Its event-driven architecture enables real-time collaboration between human developers and AI agents, while the modular panel structure supports diverse workflows from project initialization to code deployment. The domain maintains strict separation from core business logic through the Tauri IPC boundary, ensuring architectural integrity while delivering responsive user experiences.
543: 
544: ---
545: 
546: ## 12. Project Manager Agent (PM Agent) Interface
547: 
548: The GUI provides a dedicated interface for post-delivery interactions through the Project Manager Agent.
549: 
550: ### 12.1 PM Agent Activation
551: 
552: The PM Agent becomes available when an iteration reaches `Completed` status:
553: - Chat mode automatically switches from `pipeline` to `pm_agent`
554: - A welcome message is displayed with quick action buttons
555: - The chat interface becomes available for natural language interaction
556: 
557: ### 12.2 PM Agent Features
558: 
559: **Welcome Actions**:
560: - 🚀 **Start Application**: Launch the generated application
561: - 📁 **Open Project Folder**: Open workspace in file explorer
562: - 📄 **View Artifacts**: Navigate to artifacts viewer
563: - 💻 **View Source Code**: Navigate to code editor
564: - 📚 **View Knowledge Base**: Navigate to knowledge panel
565: 
566: **Conversational Interactions**:
567: - Ask questions about the project (technology stack, architecture, etc.)
568: - Request code modifications → Triggers `goto_stage` to Coding
569: - Request new features → Triggers `create_iteration` with inheritance
570: - Save decisions to project memory
571: 
572: ### 12.3 PM Agent State Management
573: 
574: **State Variables** (in `useAgentStore`):
575: - `pmMessages[]`: Message history for PM Agent chat
576: - `pmProcessing`: Boolean indicating PM Agent is processing a request
577: - `chatMode`: Current chat mode (`pipeline` | `pm_agent` | `disabled`)
578: 
579: **Events**:
580: | Event | Purpose |
581: |-------|---------|
582: | `pm_message` | PM Agent response message |
583: | `pm_actions` | Actionable buttons for user |
584: | `agent_streaming` | Real-time streaming from PM Agent |
585: | `iteration_created` | New iteration created via PM Agent |
586: 
587: ### 12.4 PM Agent Commands
588: 
589: | Command | Parameters | Description |
590: |---------|------------|-------------|
591: | `pm_send_message` | `iteration_id`, `message`, `history` | Send message to PM Agent |
592: | `pm_restart_iteration` | `iteration_id`, `target_stage` | Restart from specific stage |
593: | `pm_get_welcome_message` | `iteration_id` | Get welcome message with actions |
594: 
595: ---
596: 
597: ## 13. Settings Panel: External Coding Agent Configuration
598: 
599: The Settings Panel (`SettingsPanel.tsx`) provides configuration for external coding agents:
600: 
601: ### 13.1 Configuration Fields
602: 
603: | Field | Type | Description |
604: |-------|------|-------------|
605: | `enabled` | Boolean | Enable/disable external agent |
606: | `agent_type` | Select | Agent type (opencode, iflow, codex, gemini, claude) |
607: | `command` | String | Command to execute (e.g., "bun") |
608: | `args` | String[] | Command arguments |
609: | `transport` | Select | Communication transport (stdio, websocket) |
610: | `workspace_path` | String | Optional workspace path override |
611: 
612: ### 13.2 Agent Types
613: 
614: ```
615: OpenCode  → bun x opencode-ai acp
616: Codex     → codex
617: Gemini    → gemini
618: Claude    → claude
619: ```
620: 
621: ### 13.3 UI Components
622: 
623: - **Enable Switch**: Toggle for external agent activation
624: - **Agent Type Selector**: Dropdown with supported agent types
625: - **Command Input**: Text input for the executable command
626: - **Arguments Input**: Tag-style multi-value input for arguments
627: - **Transport Selector**: stdio or websocket options
628: - **Test Connection Button**: Validates LLM configuration
629: 
630: ---
631: 
632: ## 14. Technology Stack Updates
633: 
634: ### 14.1 Current Stack (Updated)
635: 
636: | Layer | Technology | Purpose |
637: |-------|-----------|---------|
638: | **UI Framework** | React 18 + TypeScript | Component-based declarative UI with type safety |
639: | **State Management** | Zustand | Lightweight centralized state with stores |
640: | **Component Library** | Ant Design 5.x | Enterprise-grade UI components |
641: | **Desktop Shell** | Tauri 1.x+ | Rust-based desktop runtime |
642: | **Code Editor** | Monaco Editor | VS Code-powered editing |
643: | **Virtualization** | react-window | Performance optimization |
644: 
645: ### 14.2 Zustand Stores
646: 
647: **useProjectStore**: Project and iteration state management
648: **useAgentStore**: Chat messages, PM messages, processing states
649: **useUIStore**: Active view, refresh triggers, UI preferences
````

### litho.docs/en/4.Deep-Exploration/Pipeline Domain.md (541 lines)

````
1: # Pipeline Domain Technical Documentation
2: 
3: **Version:** 1.0  
4: **Module:** `cowork-core::pipeline`  
5: **Last Updated:** 2026-02-14 05:14:32 (UTC)
6: 
7: ## 1. Overview
8: 
9: The Pipeline Domain is the central orchestration engine of Cowork Forge, implementing a structured 7-stage AI-driven software development workflow. It transforms natural language requirements into production-ready code through an iterative, validation-gated pipeline that combines autonomous AI agents with human oversight.
10: 
11: ### 1.1 Core Value Proposition
12: - **Structured Workflow**: Enforces architectural rigor through sequential stages (Idea → PRD → Design → Plan → Coding → Check → Delivery)
13: - **Quality Assurance**: Actor-Critic pattern with AI validation and Human-in-the-Loop (HITL) confirmation gates
14: - **Extensibility**: Strategy pattern enabling custom stage implementations while maintaining consistent execution semantics
15: - **Dual Interface Support**: Unified execution framework supporting both CLI automation and GUI interactions via the `InteractiveBackend` abstraction
16: 
17: ### 1.2 Domain Boundaries
18: The Pipeline Domain operates as a **Core Business Domain** within the hexagonal architecture, depending on:
19: - **Domain Logic**: For `Project` and `Iteration` entity management
20: - **Tools Domain**: For agent capabilities (file operations, data CRUD, validation)
21: - **LLM Integration**: For AI agent orchestration via `adk-rust`
22: - **Interaction Domain**: For human feedback collection and progress streaming
23: 
24: ## 2. Architectural Design
25: 
26: ### 2.1 High-Level Architecture
27: 
28: ```mermaid
29: flowchart TB
30:     subgraph PipelineDomain["Pipeline Domain"]
31:         Controller["Pipeline Controller<br/>(mod.rs)"]
32:         Executor["Stage Executor<br/>(stage_executor.rs)"]
33:         
34:         subgraph Stages["Stage Implementations"]
35:             Idea["IdeaStage"]
36:             PRD["PrdStage"]
37:             Design["DesignStage"]
38:             Plan["PlanStage"]
39:             Coding["CodingStage"]
40:             Check["CheckStage"]
41:             Delivery["DeliveryStage"]
42:         end
43:         
44:         subgraph Instructions["Agent Instructions"]
45:             Actor["Actor Prompts<br/>(Generation)"]
46:             Critic["Critic Prompts<br/>(Validation)"]
47:         end
48:     end
49:     
50:     subgraph ADK["ADK Framework"]
51:         Agent["Agent Runtime"]
52:         Context["InvocationContext"]
53:         Session["Session Management"]
54:     end
55:     
56:     subgraph External["External Systems"]
57:         LLM["LLM APIs"]
58:         Storage["Artifact Storage<br/>(.cowork-v2/)"]
59:         UI["InteractiveBackend<br/>(CLI/GUI)"]
60:     end
61:     
62:     Controller -->|Orchestrates| Executor
63:     Executor -->|Executes| Stages
64:     Stages -->|Uses| Instructions
65:     Executor -->|Creates| Agent
66:     Agent -->|Calls| LLM
67:     Agent -->|Emits| UI
68:     Agent -->|Writes| Storage
69: ```
70: 
71: ### 2.2 Design Patterns
72: 
73: | Pattern | Implementation | Purpose |
74: |---------|---------------|---------|
75: | **Strategy** | `Stage` trait with 7 concrete implementations | Polymorphic stage execution allowing uniform treatment of diverse stage logic |
76: | **Actor-Critic** | Dual-agent loops for PRD, Design, Plan, Coding stages | Quality validation through generation (Actor) and critique (Critic) cycles |
77: | **Template Method** | `execute_stage_with_instruction()` | Common execution framework with stage-specific customization points |
78: | **Bridge** | `StageExecutor` bridging domain logic with `adk-rust` | Decoupling of pipeline orchestration from agent framework specifics |
79: | **State Machine** | `StageResult` variants (Success, Failed, Paused, NeedsRevision) | Explicit handling of stage execution outcomes and transitions |
80: 
81: ## 3. Core Components
82: 
83: ### 3.1 Stage Trait Abstraction
84: 
85: The `Stage` trait defines the contract for all pipeline stages, enabling polymorphic execution while maintaining type safety:
86: 
87: ```rust
88: #[async_trait]
89: pub trait Stage: Send + Sync {
90:     /// Unique stage identifier (e.g., "idea", "prd", "coding")
91:     fn name(&self) -> &str;
92:     
93:     /// Human-readable description of stage purpose
94:     fn description(&self) -> &str;
95:     
96:     /// Determines if HITL confirmation is required before proceeding
97:     fn needs_confirmation(&self) -> bool;
98:     
99:     /// Primary execution entry point
100:     async fn execute(
101:         &self, 
102:         ctx: &PipelineContext, 
103:         interaction: &Arc<dyn InteractiveBackend>
104:     ) -> Result<StageResult>;
105:     
106:     /// Feedback-driven regeneration entry point
107:     async fn execute_with_feedback(
108:         &self, 
109:         ctx: &PipelineContext, 
110:         interaction: &Arc<dyn InteractiveBackend>,
111:         feedback: &str
112:     ) -> Result<StageResult>;
113: }
114: ```
115: 
116: **Key Design Decisions:**
117: - **Zero-Sized Types**: All stage implementations (`IdeaStage`, `PrdStage`, etc.) are unit structs with no fields, delegating all behavior to the shared executor with stage-specific constants
118: - **Async Trait**: Uses `#[async_trait]` for async execution support across stage boundaries
119: - **Feedback Loop**: Explicit `execute_with_feedback()` method enables iterative refinement without stage-specific logic duplication
120: 
121: ### 3.2 Stage Implementations
122: 
123: Seven concrete stage implementations provide the workflow structure:
124: 
125: | Stage | Type | Confirmation | Agent Pattern | Output Artifact |
126: |-------|------|-------------|---------------|-----------------|
127: | **Idea** | Individual | Yes | Single Agent | `idea.md` |
128: | **PRD** | Loop | Yes | Actor-Critic | `prd.md` |
129: | **Design** | Loop | Yes | Actor-Critic | `design.md` |
130: | **Plan** | Loop | Yes | Actor-Critic | `plan.md` |
131: | **Coding** | Loop | Yes | Actor-Critic | Code files |
132: | **Check** | Individual | No | Single Agent | `check_report.md` |
133: | **Delivery** | Individual | No | Single Agent | `delivery_report.md` |
134: 
135: **Implementation Pattern:**
136: Each stage is a zero-sized type implementing the `Stage` trait:
137: ```rust
138: pub struct DesignStage;
139: 
140: #[async_trait]
141: impl Stage for DesignStage {
142:     fn name(&self) -> &str { "design" }
143:     fn needs_confirmation(&self) -> bool { true }
144:     
145:     async fn execute(&self, ctx: &PipelineContext, interaction: &Arc<dyn InteractiveBackend>) -> Result<StageResult> {
146:         execute_stage_with_instruction(
147:             ctx, 
148:             interaction, 
149:             "design", 
150:             DESIGN_ACTOR_INSTRUCTION,
151:             None
152:         ).await
153:     }
154:     // ... execute_with_feedback implementation
155: }
156: ```
157: 
158: ### 3.3 Stage Executor
159: 
160: The `StageExecutor` (`stage_executor.rs`) serves as the **Adapter** between the pipeline domain and the `adk-rust` agent framework:
161: 
162: **Responsibilities:**
163: 1. **Agent Lifecycle Management**: Creates stage-specific agents (`create_idea_agent_with_id`, `create_prd_loop_with_id`, etc.)
164: 2. **Context Preparation**: Builds `InvocationContext` with project state, iteration metadata, and historical context
165: 3. **Prompt Engineering**: Constructs contextual prompts via `build_prompt()` combining instructions, feedback, and memory
166: 4. **Stream Processing**: Manages real-time LLM output streaming with event extraction and UI propagation
167: 5. **Artifact Coordination**: Persists generated content to `.cowork-v2/iterations/{id}/artifacts/{stage}.md`
168: 
169: **ADK Integration:**
170: Implements custom traits to bridge the domain model with the agent framework:
171: - `SimpleInvocationContext`: Provides agent configuration, memory access, and run configuration
172: - `SimpleSession`: Manages conversation history and state persistence
173: - `SimpleState`: Key-value storage for agent state across streaming chunks
174: 
175: ### 3.4 Iteration Executor (Modular Architecture)
176: 
177: The `IterationExecutor` adopts a modular architecture located in `pipeline/executor/`:
178: 
179: ```
180: executor/
181: ├── mod.rs              # Main executor entry, iteration lifecycle management
182: ├── interaction_ext.rs  # Interaction extensions (ConfirmationAction enum)
183: ├── knowledge.rs        # Knowledge generation and injection logic
184: └── workspace.rs        # Workspace preparation and inheritance management
185: ```
186: 
187: **Module Responsibilities:**
188: 
189: | Module | Responsibility |
190: |--------|----------------|
191: | **mod.rs** | Iteration lifecycle management, stage execution loop, error handling and retry logic |
192: | **interaction_ext.rs** | `ConfirmationAction` enum definition, extending `InteractiveBackend` confirmation capabilities |
193: | **knowledge.rs** | Document summary generation, iteration knowledge generation, project knowledge injection, knowledge regeneration |
194: | **workspace.rs** | Workspace preparation, inheritance mode implementation (Full/Partial/None), artifact existence checking |
195: 
196: **Knowledge Injection Mechanism (knowledge.rs):**
197: - Preferred: Use knowledge summary when available
198: - Fallback: Load artifacts directly from base iteration if knowledge summary is missing
199: - Supports project context injection into evolution iterations
200: 
201: **Inheritance Workflow (workspace.rs):**
202: - `InheritanceMode::None`: Fresh start, no file inheritance
203: - `InheritanceMode::Partial`: Copy only code files, skip artifacts directory
204: - `InheritanceMode::Full`: Complete copy of all files and artifacts
205: 
206: ### 3.4 Agent Instructions
207: 
208: Located in `crates/cowork-core/src/instructions/`, this subsystem contains approximately **2,000 lines of prompt engineering** defining AI agent behavior:
209: 
210: **Structure:**
211: - **Actor Instructions**: Define generation behavior, output formats, and tool usage patterns
212: - **Critic Instructions**: Define validation criteria, quality standards, and feedback generation
213: - **Tool Schemas**: Embedded function calling definitions for `save_idea`, `get_requirements`, `update_task_status`, `goto_stage`, etc.
214: 
215: **Example Flow (Design Stage):**
216: 1. **Design Actor** generates architecture documents using `DESIGN_ACTOR_INSTRUCTION`
217: 2. **Design Critic** validates against standards using `DESIGN_CRITIC_INSTRUCTION`
218: 3. If validation fails, Critic provides feedback triggering regeneration loop
219: 4. Upon approval, final artifact persisted to storage
220: 
221: ## 4. Execution Flow
222: 
223: ### 4.1 Standard Execution Sequence
224: 
225: ```mermaid
226: sequenceDiagram
227:     participant PC as Pipeline Controller
228:     participant Stage as Stage Trait
229:     participant SE as Stage Executor
230:     participant Agent as ADK Agent
231:     participant LLM as LLM Provider
232:     participant UI as InteractiveBackend
233:     
234:     PC->>Stage: execute(ctx, interaction)
235:     
236:     alt needs_confirmation() == true
237:         Stage->>UI: request_confirmation()
238:         UI-->>Stage: UserResponse (Pass/Edit/Feedback)
239:     end
240:     
241:     Stage->>SE: execute_stage_with_instruction()
242:     SE->>SE: load_config()
243:     SE->>SE: create_llm_client()
244:     
245:     alt Stage Type
246:         SE->>Agent: create_*_agent_with_id()
247:     end
248:     
249:     SE->>SE: build_prompt(ctx, stage, feedback)
250:     SE->>UI: show_message_with_context(status)
251:     
252:     SE->>Agent: agent.run(invocation_ctx)
253:     Agent->>LLM: Streaming Request
254:     
255:     loop Stream Processing
256:         LLM-->>Agent: Event Chunk
257:         Agent-->>SE: Stream Event
258:         SE->>SE: extract_text_from_event()
259:         SE->>UI: send_streaming(text)
260:         SE->>SE: Accumulate generated_text
261:     end
262:     
263:     SE->>Storage: Save Artifact
264:     SE-->>Stage: StageResult::Success
265:     Stage-->>PC: Continue/Complete
266: ```
267: 
268: ### 4.2 Actor-Critic Loop (Complex Stages)
269: 
270: For PRD, Design, Plan, and Coding stages, the system implements an **Actor-Critic Pattern**:
271: 
272: ```mermaid
273: flowchart LR
274:     Actor["Actor Agent<br/>Generates Artifact"] --> Critic["Critic Agent<br/>Validates Quality"]
275:     Critic -->|Approved| Output["Final Output"]
276:     Critic -->|Needs Work| Revision["Regenerate with Feedback"]
277:     Revision --> Actor
278: ```
279: 
280: **Implementation Details:**
281: - **Loop Agents**: Specialized agent constructors (`create_prd_loop_with_id`, etc.) instantiate paired Actor-Critic agents
282: - **Iteration Control**: Maximum iteration limits prevent infinite regeneration loops
283: - **Feedback Integration**: Critic critiques are passed as feedback to the Actor's next generation attempt
284: 
285: ### 4.3 Human-in-the-Loop Integration
286: 
287: Critical stages (Idea, PRD, Design, Plan, Coding) implement confirmation gates:
288: 
289: **Confirmation Points:**
290: 1. **Pre-execution**: User approves proceeding to stage
291: 2. **Post-execution**: User reviews generated artifact before proceeding
292: 
293: **Interaction Modes:**
294: - **Pass**: Approve and continue to next stage
295: - **Edit**: Open artifact in external editor, reload modified content
296: - **Feedback**: Provide textual feedback triggering `execute_with_feedback()`
297: 
298: **Technical Implementation:**
299: ```rust
300: // In Stage trait implementations
301: async fn execute(&self, ctx: &PipelineContext, interaction: &Arc<dyn InteractiveBackend>) -> Result<StageResult> {
302:     // Stage-specific execution
303:     let result = execute_stage_with_instruction(...).await?;
304:     
305:     // HITL integration via InteractiveBackend trait
306:     if self.needs_confirmation() {
307:         interaction.show_message_with_context(
308:             MessageLevel::Info,
309:             "Stage complete. Review artifact before continuing.",
310:             MessageContext::new(self.name(), ctx.iteration.id)
311:         ).await?;
312:     }
313:     
314:     Ok(result)
315: }
316: ```
317: 
318: ## 5. Data Models & Context
319: 
320: ### 5.1 PipelineContext
321: 
322: The execution context carrying state across stages:
323: 
324: ```rust
325: pub struct PipelineContext {
326:     pub project: Project,           // Project aggregate root
327:     pub iteration: Iteration,       // Current iteration entity
328:     pub workspace_path: PathBuf,    // Sandbox directory for artifacts
329:     // Additional configuration and state...
330: }
331: ```
332: 
333: ### 5.2 StageResult
334: 
335: Explicit result type enforcing state machine semantics:
336: 
337: ```rust
338: pub enum StageResult {
339:     Success(Option<String>),    // Artifact content (optional)
340:     Failed(String),             // Error message
341:     Paused,                     // Awaiting external input
342:     NeedsRevision(String),      // Feedback for regeneration
343: }
344: ```
345: 
346: ### 5.3 Artifact Storage
347: 
348: Artifacts follow a structured storage convention:
349: ```
350: .cowork-v2/
351: └── iterations/
352:     └── {iteration_id}/
353:         └── artifacts/
354:             ├── idea.md
355:             ├── prd.md
356:             ├── design.md
357:             ├── plan.md
358:             ├── check.md
359:             └── delivery.md
360: ```
361: 
362: ## 6. Integration Points
363: 
364: ### 6.1 Interface Abstraction
365: 
366: The `InteractiveBackend` trait (`crates/cowork-core/src/interaction/`) abstracts UI concerns:
367: 
368: ```rust
369: #[async_trait]
370: pub trait InteractiveBackend: Send + Sync {
371:     async fn show_message(&self, level: MessageLevel, message: &str);
372:     async fn show_message_with_context(&self, level: MessageLevel, message: &str, context: MessageContext);
373:     async fn send_streaming(&self, content: &str, display_name: &str, is_complete: bool);
374:     async fn request_confirmation(&self, content: &str) -> Result<UserResponse>;
375: }
376: ```
377: 
378: **Implementations:**
379: - **CLI Backend**: Terminal UI with colored output using `dialoguer`
380: - **Tauri Backend**: Event emission to React frontend via Tauri's IPC system
381: 
382: ### 6.2 LLM Integration
383: 
384: The Stage Executor interacts with the **LLM Integration Domain** through:
385: - **Rate-Limited Client**: All requests pass through `RateLimitedLlm` (30 req/min, concurrency=1)
386: - **Configuration Loading**: `load_config()` resolves LLM settings from TOML files or environment variables
387: - **Model Selection**: Stage-specific model configurations (e.g., stronger models for Design, faster models for Check)
388: 
389: ### 6.3 Tool System Integration
390: 
391: Agents invoke tools from the **Tools Domain** during execution:
392: - **File Tools**: `ReadFileTool`, `WriteFileTool` for artifact manipulation
393: - **Data Tools**: `GetRequirementsTool`, `UpdateTaskStatusTool` for project state management
394: - **HITL Tools**: `ReviewAndEditContentTool` for human validation
395: - **Memory Tools**: `QueryMemoryTool` for historical context retrieval
396: 
397: ## 7. Configuration & Extensibility
398: 
399: ### 7.1 Adding New Stages
400: 
401: To implement a new pipeline stage:
402: 
403: 1. **Create Stage Struct**:
404: ```rust
405: pub struct CustomStage;
406: ```
407: 
408: 2. **Implement Stage Trait**:
409: ```rust
410: #[async_trait]
411: impl Stage for CustomStage {
412:     fn name(&self) -> &str { "custom" }
413:     fn needs_confirmation(&self) -> bool { true }
414:     
415:     async fn execute(&self, ctx: &PipelineContext, interaction: &Arc<dyn InteractiveBackend>) -> Result<StageResult> {
416:         execute_stage_with_instruction(
417:             ctx,
418:             interaction,
419:             "custom",
420:             CUSTOM_INSTRUCTION,
421:             None
422:         ).await
423:     }
424: }
425: ```
426: 
427: 3. **Define Instructions**:
428: Create `crates/cowork-core/src/instructions/custom.rs` with `CUSTOM_INSTRUCTION` constant.
429: 
430: 4. **Register in Pipeline**:
431: Add to `get_all_stages()` in `crates/cowork-core/src/pipeline/mod.rs`.
432: 
433: ### 7.2 Customizing Agent Behavior
434: 
435: Modify stage behavior through:
436: - **Instruction Updates**: Edit prompt constants in `instructions/` modules
437: - **Tool Injection**: Extend agent capabilities by adding tools to agent constructors in `stage_executor.rs`
438: - **Context Enrichment**: Modify `build_prompt()` to include additional domain context
439: 
440: ## 8. Error Handling & Resilience
441: 
442: ### 8.1 Error Categories
443: 
444: | Category | Handling Strategy | Recovery |
445: |----------|------------------|----------|
446: | **LLM Errors** | Retry with exponential backoff | StageResult::Failed |
447: | **Tool Execution** | Validation + rollback | StageResult::Failed |
448: | **User Cancellation** | Graceful shutdown | StageResult::Paused |
449: | **Validation Failures** | Actor-Critic loop | StageResult::NeedsRevision |
450: 
451: ### 8.2 Streaming Resilience
452: 
453: - **Partial Content Persistence**: Streamed chunks are accumulated in memory and persisted only upon successful completion
454: - **Timeout Handling**: Long-running generations have configurable timeouts
455: - **Interruption Recovery**: Support for pausing and resuming at stage boundaries
456: 
457: ## 9. Performance Considerations
458: 
459: ### 9.1 Concurrency Model
460: - **Single Pipeline Execution**: Only one pipeline runs per iteration (enforced by Iteration state machine)
461: - **Sequential Stages**: Stages execute serially to maintain dependency ordering
462: - **Async I/O**: All file operations and LLM requests are non-blocking (Tokio runtime)
463: 
464: ### 9.2 Resource Management
465: - **Memory**: Streaming processing prevents loading large artifacts entirely into memory
466: - **Rate Limiting**: Global semaphore ensures compliance with LLM API quotas
467: - **Storage**: Artifacts written atomically to prevent corruption
468: 
469: ## 10. Security Considerations
470: 
471: ### 10.1 Path Validation
472: All file operations validate paths against the iteration workspace:
473: ```rust
474: // Implicit in File Tools via Security domain
475: assert!(path.starts_with(&ctx.workspace_path));
476: ```
477: 
478: ### 10.2 Sandboxing
479: - **Workspace Confinement**: Agents can only access files within `.cowork-v2/iterations/{id}/`
480: - **Command Restrictions**: `RunCommandTool` validates commands against allow-lists
481: - **No Network Access**: Agents cannot initiate outbound network requests (except via LLM client)
482: 
483: ## 11. Testing Strategy
484: 
485: ### 11.1 Unit Testing
486: - **Stage Logic**: Test individual stage behaviors with mock `InteractiveBackend`
487: - **Prompt Validation**: Verify instruction constants compile and contain required placeholders
488: - **Result Handling**: Test `StageResult` state transitions
489: 
490: ### 11.2 Integration Testing
491: - **End-to-End Pipeline**: Execute full 7-stage workflows with stubbed LLM responses
492: - **HITL Simulation**: Mock user inputs for confirmation gates
493: - **Artifact Verification**: Validate generated files match expected schemas
494: 
495: ## 12. Future Enhancements
496: 
497: ### 12.1 Planned Improvements
498: - **Parallel Stage Execution**: Independent stages (Check + Delivery) could execute concurrently
499: - **Checkpoint Recovery**: Resume from arbitrary stage after system restart
500: - **Dynamic Stage Injection**: Runtime stage registration for plugin architecture
501: 
502: ### 12.2 Architectural Evolution
503: - **Event Sourcing**: Consider event-sourced pipeline state for complex workflow visualization
504: - **Distributed Execution**: Remote agent execution for compute-intensive coding stages
505: 
506: ---
507: 
508: ## Appendix A: File Structure
509: 
510: ```
511: crates/cowork-core/src/pipeline/
512: ├── mod.rs                    # Pipeline controller and stage orchestration
513: ├── stage_executor.rs         # ADK integration and execution framework
514: ├── executor/                 # Modular iteration executor
515: │   ├── mod.rs               # Main executor entry, iteration lifecycle management
516: │   ├── interaction_ext.rs   # Interaction extensions, ConfirmationAction enum
517: │   ├── knowledge.rs         # Knowledge generation and injection logic
518: │   └── workspace.rs         # Workspace preparation and inheritance management
519: └── ...
520: 
521: crates/cowork-core/src/instructions/
522: ├── idea.rs                  # Idea agent prompts
523: ├── prd.rs                   # PRD Actor/Critic prompts
524: ├── design.rs                # Design Actor/Critic prompts
525: ├── plan.rs                  # Plan Actor/Critic prompts
526: ├── coding.rs                # Coding Actor/Critic prompts
527: ├── check.rs                 # Check agent prompts
528: ├── delivery.rs              # Delivery agent prompts
529: ├── legacy_project_analyzer.rs  # Legacy Project Analyzer prompts
530: └── ...
531: ```
532: 
533: ## Appendix B: Glossary
534: 
535: - **ADK**: Agent Development Kit (Rust framework for LLM agent orchestration)
536: - **Actor-Critic**: ML pattern where one agent generates content (Actor) and another validates it (Critic)
537: - **Genesis Iteration**: Initial project creation workflow (all 7 stages)
538: - **Evolution Iteration**: Subsequent iteration building on previous work
539: - **HITL**: Human-in-the-Loop (human validation gates)
540: - **Stage**: Discrete pipeline phase with specific inputs and outputs
541: - **Artifact**: Persistent output generated by a stage (Markdown files, code, etc.)
````

116:         user_input: &str,

119:     ) -> Result<IterationIntent> {

121:         
122:         let prompt = format!(
123:             r#"你是一个项目迭代助手，负责分析用户的意图并决定下一步行动。
124: 
125: 当前状态:
126: - Session 状态: {}

136: 
137: 1. ContinueDevelopment - 继续开发当前项目（用户想要继续完善项目）
138: 2. StartNewFeature - 开发新功能（用户想要添加新功能）
139: 3. FixBug - 修复问题（用户报告了 bug）
140: 4. Refactor - 重构代码（用户想要重构）
141: 5. NewProject - 创建全新项目（用户想要开始新项目）
142: 6. Clarification - 需要更多信息（用户意图不明确）
143: 
144: 请以 JSON 格式返回你的分析结果:
145: {{
146:   "intent_type": "ContinueDevelopment",
147:   "confidence": 0.9,
148:   "reasoning": "用户说'继续优化首页性能'，这表明用户想要继续改进当前项目",
149:   "suggested_action": "modify",
150:   "related_features": ["首页", "性能优化"]
151: }}
152: 
153: 注意：
154: - intent_type 必须是上面列出的6种之一
155: - confidence 是0到1之间的浮点数
156: - suggested_action 可以是 "modify", "resume", "new", "ask_clarification", "ignore"
157: - reasoning 简洁解释你的判断
158: - related_features 列出相关的功能或模块
159: "#,
160:             current_session_status.status,
161:             current_session_status.session_type,
162:             current_session_status.created_at,
163:             project_context_json,
164:             user_input
165:         );
166:         
167:         let response = self.llm.generate(&prompt)?;
168:         
169:         
170:         let intent: IterationIntent = serde_json::from_str(&response)
171:             .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!("Failed to parse intent response: {}. Response was: {}", e, response))?;
172:         
173:         Ok(intent)
174:     }
175:     
176:     
177:     pub async fn generate_modify_suggestion(
178:         &self,
179:         project_context: &ProjectContext,
180:         user_input: &str,
181:     ) -> Result<ModifySuggestion> {
182:         let project_context_json = serde_json::to_string_pretty(project_context)?;
183:         
184:         let prompt = format!(
185:             r#"用户请求对项目进行修改。
186: 
187: 项目上下文:
188: {}
189: 
190: 用户输入: {}
191: 
192: 请分析这个修改请求，并提供详细的修改建议，包括:
193: 
194: 1. 修改类型识别（feature_addition, feature_modification, bug_fix, refactor）
195: 2. 受影响模块分析
196: 3. 实施计划（分步骤）
197: 4. 风险评估
198: 
199: 请以 JSON 格式返回:
200: {{
201:   "modification_type": "feature_addition",
202:   "title": "添加用户评论功能",
203:   "affected_modules": ["frontend", "backend", "database"],
204:   "implementation_plan": [
205:     "1. 设计评论数据模型",
206:     "2. 实现后端 API",
207:     "3. 实现前端 UI",
208:     "4. 集成到现有系统"
209:   ],
210:   "risk_assessment": {{
211:     "risk_level": "medium",
212:     "risks": [
213:       "可能需要数据库迁移",
214:       "影响现有功能"
215:     ],
216:     "mitigation_strategies": [
217:       "使用数据库迁移工具",
218:       "充分测试现有功能"
219:     ]
220:   }},
221:   "estimated_effort": "2-3天",
222:   "confidence": 0.85
223: }}
224: 
225: 注意：
226: - modification_type 可以是 "feature_addition", "feature_modification", "bug_fix", "refactor"
227: - risk_level 可以是 "low", "medium", "high", "critical"
228: - estimated_effort 使用描述性文本，如 "2-3天", "1周" 等
229: - confidence 是0到1之间的浮点数
230: "#,
231:             project_context_json,
232:             user_input
233:         );
234:         
235:         let response = self.llm.generate(&prompt)?;
236:         
237:         
238:         let suggestion: ModifySuggestion = serde_json::from_str(&response)
239:             .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!("Failed to parse suggestion response: {}. Response was: {}", e, response))?;
240:         
241:         Ok(suggestion)
242:     }
243:     
244:     
245:     pub async fn generate_clarification_questions(
246:         &self,
247:         user_input: &str,
248:         project_context: &ProjectContext,
249:     ) -> Result<Vec<String>> {
250:         let project_context_json = serde_json::to_string_pretty(project_context)?;
251:         
252:         let prompt = format!(
253:             r#"用户的请求不够明确，需要更多信息。
254: 
255: 项目上下文:
256: {}
257: 
258: 用户输入: {}
259: 
260: 请生成2-3个澄清问题，帮助更好地理解用户的需求。
261: 
262: 请以 JSON 格式返回:
263: {{
264:   "questions": [
265:     "你想修改哪个功能？",
266:     "具体要实现什么效果？",
267:     "有没有参考的例子？"
268:   ]
269: }}
270: 
271: 注意：
272: - 问题要简洁明了
273: - 问题要能帮助缩小需求范围
274: - 最多3个问题
275: "#,
276:             project_context_json,
277:             user_input
278:         );
279:         
280:         let response = self.llm.generate(&prompt)?;
281:         
282:         
283:         let result: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_str(&response)?;
284:         
285:         let questions: Vec<String> = result["questions"]
286:             .as_array()
287:             .unwrap_or(&vec![])
288:             .iter()
289:             .filter_map(|q| q.as_str().map(|s| s.to_string()))
290:             .collect();
291:         
292:         Ok(questions)
293:     }
294: }
295: ⋮----
296: MockLlmProvider
297: ⋮----
298: {
299:     fn generate(&self, _prompt: &str) -> Result<String> {
300:         Ok(r#"{
301:   "intent_type": "ContinueDevelopment",
302:   "confidence": 0.9,
303:   "reasoning": "Mock response",
304:   "suggested_action": "modify",
305:   "related_features": []
306: }"#.to_string())
307:     }
308: }
309: ⋮----
310: test_iteration_intent_serialize
311: ⋮----
312: ()
313: ⋮----
314: test_modify_suggestion_serialize
315: ⋮----
316: ()
```

248: {
249:     
250:     pub fn rest_api(id: impl Into<String>, name: impl Into<String>, base_url: impl Into<String>) -> Self {
251:         Self {
252:             id: id.into(),
253:             name: name.into(),
254:             description: None,
255:             integration_type: IntegrationType::RestApi,
256:             connection: ConnectionConfig {
257:                 base_url: Some(base_url.into()),
258:                 endpoints: HashMap::new(),
259:                 headers: HashMap::new(),
260:                 params: HashMap::new(),
261:             },
262:             auth: None,
263:             retry: RetryConfig::default(),
264:             timeout_secs: 30,
265:             enabled: true,
266:             tags: Vec::new(),
267:             metadata: HashMap::new(),
268:         }
269:     }
270:     
271:     
272:     pub fn with_endpoint(mut self, name: impl Into<String>, endpoint: EndpointConfig) -> Self {
273:         self.connection.endpoints.insert(name.into(), endpoint);
274:         self
275:     }
276:     
277:     
278:     pub fn with_auth(mut self, auth: AuthConfig) -> Self {
279:         self.auth = Some(auth);
280:         self
281:     }
282:     
283:     
284:     pub fn with_api_key_env(mut self, env_var: impl Into<String>) -> Self {

288:         });

296:             credentials: CredentialSource::EnvVar { name: env_var.into() },
297:         });
298:         self
299:     }

155: {

159:             id: id.into(),
160:             name: name.into(),

164:             actor_critic: None,

171:             metadata: HashMap::new(),
172:         }
173:     }
174:     
175:     
176:     pub fn actor_critic(
177:         id: impl Into<String>, 
178:         name: impl Into<String>, 
179:         actor: impl Into<String>, 
180:         critic: impl Into<String>
181:     ) -> Self {
182:         Self {
183:             id: id.into(),
184:             name: name.into(),
185:             description: None,
186:             stage_type: StageType::ActorCritic,
187:             agent: None,
188:             actor_critic: Some(ActorCriticStageConfig {
189:                 actor: actor.into(),
190:                 critic: critic.into(),
191:                 max_iterations: 1,
192:             }),
193:             needs_confirmation: false,
194:             artifacts: Vec::new(),
195:             hooks: Vec::new(),
196:             timeout_secs: None,
197:             retry: StageRetryConfig::default(),
198:             tags: Vec::new(),
199:             metadata: HashMap::new(),
200:         }
201:     }
202:     
203:     
204:     pub fn with_confirmation(mut self) -> Self {
205:         self.needs_confirmation = true;
206:         self
207:     }
208:     
209:     
210:     pub fn with_artifact(mut self, path: impl Into<String>, required: bool) -> Self {
211:         self.artifacts.push(ArtifactConfig {
212:             path: path.into(),
213:             required,
214:             description: None,
215:         });
216:         self
217:     }
218:     
219:     
220:     pub fn with_hook(mut self, hook: HookConfig) -> Self {
221:         self.hooks.push(hook);
222:         self
223:     }
224: }

### crates/cowork-core/src/domain/memory.rs (377 lines)

```
1: ProjectMemory
2: ⋮----
3: {
4:     pub decisions: Vec<Decision>,
5:     pub patterns: Vec<Pattern>,
6:     pub context: ProjectContext,
7:     
8:     pub iteration_knowledge: std::collections::HashMap<String, IterationKnowledge>,
9: }
10: ⋮----
11: ProjectMemory
12: ⋮----
13: {
14:     pub fn new() -> Self {
15:         Self::default()
16:     }
17: 
18:     pub fn add_decision(&mut self, decision: Decision) {
19:         self.decisions.push(decision);
20:     }
21: 
22:     pub fn add_pattern(&mut self, pattern: Pattern) {
23:         self.patterns.push(pattern);
24:     }
25: 
26:     
27:     pub fn query_decisions(&self, keyword: &str) -> Vec<&Decision> {
28:         let keyword_lower = keyword.to_lowercase();
29:         self.decisions
30:             .iter()
31:             .filter(|d| {
32:                 d.title.to_lowercase().contains(&keyword_lower)
33:                     || d.context.to_lowercase().contains(&keyword_lower)
34:             })
35:             .collect()
36:     }
37: 
38:     
39:     pub fn query_patterns(&self, tag: &str) -> Vec<&Pattern> {
40:         let tag_lower = tag.to_lowercase();
41:         self.patterns
42:             .iter()
43:             .filter(|p| p.tags.iter().any(|t| t.to_lowercase() == tag_lower))
44:             .collect()
45:     }
46: 
47:     
48:     pub fn save_iteration_knowledge(&mut self, knowledge: IterationKnowledge) {
49:         self.iteration_knowledge.insert(knowledge.iteration_id.clone(), knowledge);
50:     }
51: 
52:     
53:     pub fn get_iteration_knowledge(&self, iteration_id: &str) -> Option<&IterationKnowledge> {
54:         self.iteration_knowledge.get(iteration_id)
55:     }
56: 
57:     
58:     pub fn remove_iteration_knowledge(&mut self, iteration_id: &str) {
59:         self.iteration_knowledge.remove(iteration_id);
60:     }
61: 
62:     
63:     pub fn cleanup_old_knowledge(&mut self, keep_count: usize) {
64:         let mut knowledge_vec: Vec<_> = self.iteration_knowledge.values().collect();
65:         
66:         
67:         knowledge_vec.sort_by_key(|k| k.iteration_number);
68:         
69:         
70:         if knowledge_vec.len() > keep_count {
71:             let to_remove = knowledge_vec.len() - keep_count;
72:             let iteration_ids: Vec<String> = knowledge_vec[..to_remove].iter().map(|k| k.iteration_id.clone()).collect();
73:             for id in iteration_ids {
74:                 self.iteration_knowledge.remove(&id);
75:             }
76:         }
77:     }
78: }
79: ⋮----

118:         Self {
119:             iteration_id,
120:             iteration_number,
121:             idea_summary: String::new(),
122:             prd_summary: String::new(),
123:             design_summary: String::new(),
124:             plan_summary: String::new(),
125:             tech_stack: Vec::new(),
126:             key_decisions: Vec::new(),
127:             key_patterns: Vec::new(),

131:         }

149: {
150:     pub fn new(
151:         title: impl Into<String>,
152:         context: impl Into<String>,
153:         decision: impl Into<String>,
154:         iteration_id: impl Into<String>,
155:     ) -> Self {
156:         let now = Utc::now();
157:         let iteration_id = iteration_id.into();
158:         Self {
159:             id: format!("dec-{}-{}", iteration_id, Uuid::new_v4()),
160:             title: title.into(),

185: {

187:         name: impl Into<String>,
188:         description: impl Into<String>,
189:         iteration_id: impl Into<String>,
190:     ) -> Self {
191:         let now = Utc::now();
192:         let iteration_id = iteration_id.into();
193:         Self {
194:             id: format!("pat-{}-{}", iteration_id, Uuid::new_v4()),
195:             name: name.into(),
196:             description: description.into(),
197:             usage: Vec::new(),
198:             tags: Vec::new(),
199:             code_example: None,
200:             iteration_id,
201:             created_at: now,
202:         }
203:     }
204: }
205: ⋮----

266:         });
267:     }
268: }

270: Insight
271: ⋮----

273:     pub stage: String,
274:     pub content: String,
275:     pub importance: Importance,
276:     pub created_at: DateTime<Utc>,
277: }
278: ⋮----
279: Issue
280: ⋮----
281: {

286:     pub resolved_at: Option<DateTime<Utc>>,

299:     Critical,
300:     Important,
301:     Normal,

305: ⋮----

316:     Project,

321: MemoryQueryType

324:     Decisions,

336: }
337: ⋮----
338: MemoryQueryResult
339: ⋮----
340: {
341:     pub fn is_empty(&self) -> bool {
342:         self.decisions.is_empty() && self.patterns.is_empty() && self.insights.is_empty()
343:     }
344: 
345:     pub fn merge(&mut self, other: MemoryQueryResult) {
346:         self.decisions.extend(other.decisions);
347:         self.patterns.extend(other.patterns);
348:         self.insights.extend(other.insights);
349:     }
350: 
351:     pub fn to_context_string(&self) -> String {
352:         let mut parts = Vec::new();
353: 
354:         if !self.decisions.is_empty() {
355:             parts.push("## Key Decisions\n".to_string());
356:             for d in &self.decisions {
357:                 parts.push(format!("- {}: {}", d.title, d.decision));
358:             }
359:         }
360: 
361:         if !self.patterns.is_empty() {
362:             parts.push("\n## Patterns\n".to_string());
363:             for p in &self.patterns {
364:                 parts.push(format!("- {}: {}", p.name, p.description));
365:             }
366:         }
367: 
368:         if !self.insights.is_empty() {
369:             parts.push("\n## Insights\n".to_string());
370:             for i in &self.insights {
371:                 parts.push(format!("- [{}] {}", i.stage, i.content));
372:             }
373:         }
374: 
375:         parts.join("\n")
376:     }
377: }
```

1: Project
2: ⋮----
3: {
4:     pub id: String,
5:     pub name: String,
6:     pub created_at: DateTime<Utc>,
7:     pub updated_at: DateTime<Utc>,
8:     pub metadata: ProjectMetadata,
9:     pub current_iteration_id: Option<String>,
10:     pub iterations: Vec<IterationSummary>,
11: }
12: ⋮----
13: Project
14: ⋮----
15: {
16:     pub fn new(name: impl Into<String>) -> Self {
17:         let now = Utc::now();
18:         Self {
19:             id: format!("proj-{}", now.timestamp()),
20:             name: name.into(),
21:             created_at: now,
22:             updated_at: now,
23:             metadata: ProjectMetadata::default(),
24:             current_iteration_id: None,
25:             iterations: Vec::new(),
26:         }
27:     }
28: 
29:     pub fn add_iteration(&mut self, summary: IterationSummary) {
30:         self.iterations.push(summary);
31:         self.updated_at = Utc::now();
32:     }
33: 
34:     pub fn set_current_iteration(&mut self, iteration_id: String) {
35:         self.current_iteration_id = Some(iteration_id);
36:         self.updated_at = Utc::now();
37:     }
38: 
39:     pub fn get_latest_completed_iteration(&self) -> Option<&IterationSummary> {
40:         self.iterations
41:             .iter()
42:             .filter(|i| i.status == IterationStatus::Completed)
43:             .max_by_key(|i| i.number)
44:     }
45: 
46:     pub fn next_iteration_number(&self) -> u32 {
47:         self.iterations.len() as u32 + 1
48:     }
49: }
50: ⋮----
51: ProjectMetadata
52: ⋮----

54:     pub tech_stack: Vec<String>,
55:     pub project_type: String,
56:     pub language: String,
57: }
58: ⋮----

66:     pub completed_stages: Vec<String>,
67:     pub created_at: DateTime<Utc>,

69: ⋮----
70: IterationStatus
71: ⋮----
72: {
73:     Draft,
74:     Running,
75:     Paused,
76:     Completed,
77:     Failed,
78: }
79: ⋮----
80: IterationStatus
81: ⋮----
82: {
83:     fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
84:         match self {
85:             IterationStatus::Draft => write!(f, "draft"),
86:             IterationStatus::Running => write!(f, "running"),
87:             IterationStatus::Paused => write!(f, "paused"),
88:             IterationStatus::Completed => write!(f, "completed"),
89:             IterationStatus::Failed => write!(f, "failed"),
90:         }
91:     }
92: }
