19: {
20:     /// Create a new analyzer
21:     pub fn new() -> Self {
22:         Self { llm_config: None }
23:     }
24: 
25:     /// Create with LLM config
26:     pub fn with_llm_config(mut self, config: LlmConfig) -> Self {
27:         self.llm_config = Some(config);
28:         self
29:     }
30: 
31:     /// Analyze project and generate runtime configuration
32:     pub async fn analyze(&self, workspace: &Path) -> Result<ProjectRuntimeConfig, String> {
33:         // 1. Collect project information
34:         let project_info = self.collect_project_info(workspace)?;
35: 
36:         // 2. Try LLM-based analysis first
37:         if let Some(config) = &self.llm_config {
38:             match self.analyze_with_llm(&project_info, config).await {
39:                 Ok(config) => {
40:                     // 3. Validate with security checker
41:                     let checker =
42:                         RuntimeSecurityChecker::new().with_project_root(workspace.to_path_buf());
43:                     let security_result = checker.check_config(&config);
44: 
45:                     if !security_result.is_safe {
46:                         return Err(format!(
47:                             "安全检查失败: {}",
48:                             security_result.errors.join("; ")
49:                         ));
50:                     }
51: 
52:                     let mut config = config;
53:                     if !security_result.warnings.is_empty() {
54:                         config.security_warnings = Some(security_result.warnings);
55:                     }
56: 
57:                     return Ok(config);
58:                 }
59:                 Err(e) => {
60:                     tracing::warn!("LLM analysis failed: {}, falling back to heuristic", e);
61:                 }
62:             }
63:         }
64: 
65:         // 4. Fallback to heuristic analysis
66:         self.analyze_with_heuristic(&project_info, workspace)
67:     }
68: 
69:     /// Collect project information for analysis
70:     fn collect_project_info(&self, workspace: &Path) -> Result<ProjectInfo, String> {
71:         let mut detected_files = Vec::new();
72:         let mut file_tree = String::new();
73: 
74:         // Collect file tree
75:         let walker = walkdir::WalkDir::new(workspace)
76:             .max_depth(4)
77:             .into_iter()
78:             .filter_entry(|e| {
79:                 let name = e.file_name().to_string_lossy();
80:                 // Skip hidden and build directories
81:                 !name.starts_with('.')
82:                     && name != "node_modules"
83:                     && name != "target"
84:                     && name != "dist"
85:                     && name != "build"
86:                     && name != "__pycache__"
87:             });
88: 
89:         for entry in walker.filter_map(|e| e.ok()) {
90:             let path = entry.path();
91:             let relative = path.strip_prefix(workspace).unwrap_or(path);
92:             let relative_str = relative.to_string_lossy().to_string();
93: 
94:             if !relative_str.is_empty() && !relative_str.starts_with('.') {
95:                 detected_files.push(relative_str.clone());
96: 
97:                 let indent = relative_str.matches('/').count() + relative_str.matches('\\').count();
98:                 let prefix = "  ".repeat(indent);
99:                 let marker = if path.is_dir() { "📁 " } else { "📄 " };
100:                 file_tree.push_str(&format!("{}{}{}\n", prefix, marker, relative_str));
101:             }
102:         }
103: 
104:         // Read package.json
105:         let package_json = self.read_json_file(&workspace.join("package.json"))?;
106: 
107:         // Read Cargo.toml
108:         let cargo_toml = fs::read_to_string(workspace.join("Cargo.toml")).ok();
109: 
110:         // Read vite.config
111:         let vite_config = self
112:             .read_config_file(&workspace.join("vite.config.js"))
113:             .or_else(|| self.read_config_file(&workspace.join("vite.config.ts")))
114:             .or_else(|| self.read_config_file(&workspace.join("vite.config.mjs")));
115: 
116:         Ok(ProjectInfo {
117:             file_tree,
118:             package_json,
119:             cargo_toml,
120:             vite_config,
121:             detected_files,
122:         })
123:     }
124: 
125:     /// Read and parse JSON file
126:     fn read_json_file(&self, path: &Path) -> Result<Option<serde_json::Value>, String> {
127:         match fs::read_to_string(path) {
128:             Ok(content) => match serde_json::from_str(&content) {
129:                 Ok(json) => Ok(Some(json)),
130:                 Err(e) => {
131:                     tracing::warn!("Failed to parse JSON {}: {}", path.display(), e);
132:                     Ok(None)
133:                 }
134:             },
135:             Err(_) => Ok(None),
136:         }
137:     }
138: 
139:     /// Read config file content
140:     fn read_config_file(&self, path: &Path) -> Option<String> {
141:         fs::read_to_string(path).ok()
142:     }
143: 
144:     /// Analyze with LLM
145:     async fn analyze_with_llm(
146:         &self,
147:         project_info: &ProjectInfo,
148:         config: &LlmConfig,
149:     ) -> Result<ProjectRuntimeConfig, String> {
150:         // Build prompt
151:         let prompt = Self::build_prompt(project_info);
152: 
153:         // Call LLM
154:         let client = reqwest::Client::new();
155:         let response = client
156:             .post(&config.api_base_url)
157:             .header("Authorization", format!("Bearer {}", config.api_key))
158:             .header("Content-Type", "application/json")
159:             .json(&serde_json::json!({
160:                 "model": config.model_name,
161:                 "messages": [
162:                     {"role": "system", "content": "You are a software project analyzer. Output ONLY JSON, no other text."},
163:                     {"role": "user", "content": prompt}
164:                 ],
165:                 "temperature": 0.1,
166:                 "max_tokens": 2000
167:             }))
168:             .send()
169:             .await
170:             .map_err(|e| format!("LLM request failed: {}", e))?;
171: 
172:         let response_json: serde_json::Value = response
173:             .json()
174:             .await
175:             .map_err(|e| format!("Failed to parse LLM response: {}", e))?;
176: 
177:         let content = response_json
178:             .get("choices")
179:             .and_then(|c| c.as_array())
180:             .and_then(|a| a.first())
181:             .and_then(|c| c.get("message"))
182:             .and_then(|m| m.get("content"))
183:             .and_then(|c| c.as_str())
184:             .ok_or("Invalid LLM response format")?;
185: 
186:         // Parse JSON from response (might be wrapped in markdown)
187:         let json_str = content
188:             .trim()
189:             .trim_start_matches("```json")
190:             .trim_start_matches("```")
191:             .trim_end_matches("```")
192:             .trim();
193: 
194:         let parsed: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_str(json_str)
195:             .map_err(|e| format!("Failed to parse LLM output: {}\nOutput: {}", e, json_str))?;
196: 
197:         // Convert to ProjectRuntimeConfig
198:         Self::parse_llm_response(&parsed)
199:     }
200: 
201:     /// Build analysis prompt
202:     fn build_prompt(info: &ProjectInfo) -> String {
203:         let package_json_content = info
204:             .package_json
205:             .as_ref()
206:             .map(|j| serde_json::to_string_pretty(j).unwrap_or_default())
207:             .unwrap_or_else(|| "N/A".to_string());
208: 
209:         let package_json_exists = if info.package_json.is_some() {
210:             "(存在)"
211:         } else {
212:             "(不存在)"
213:         };
214: 
215:         let cargo_toml_content = info.cargo_toml.as_deref().unwrap_or("N/A");
216:         let cargo_toml_exists = if info.cargo_toml.is_some() {
217:             "(存在)"
218:         } else {
219:             "(不存在)"
220:         };
221: 
222:         let vite_config_content = info.vite_config.as_deref().unwrap_or("N/A");
223:         let vite_config_exists = if info.vite_config.is_some() {
224:             "(存在)"
225:         } else {
226:             "(不存在)"
227:         };
228: 
229:         ANALYSIS_PROMPT
230:             .replace("{file_tree}", &info.file_tree)
231:             .replace("{package_json_exists}", package_json_exists)
232:             .replace("{package_json_content}", &package_json_content)
233:             .replace("{cargo_toml_exists}", cargo_toml_exists)
234:             .replace("{cargo_toml_content}", cargo_toml_content)
235:             .replace("{vite_config_exists}", vite_config_exists)
236:             .replace("{vite_config_content}", vite_config_content)
237:     }
238: 
239:     /// Parse LLM response to ProjectRuntimeConfig
240:     fn parse_llm_response(json: &serde_json::Value) -> Result<ProjectRuntimeConfig, String> {
241:         let runtime_type_str = json
242:             .get("runtime_type")
243:             .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
244:             .unwrap_or("unknown");
245: 
246:         let runtime_type = match runtime_type_str {
247:             "react_vite" | "react" => RuntimeType::ReactVite,
248:             "react_cra" => RuntimeType::ReactCra,
249:             "vue_vite" => RuntimeType::VueVite,
250:             "vue" => RuntimeType::VueCli,
251:             "solid" => RuntimeType::SolidVite,
252:             "svelte" => RuntimeType::SvelteVite,
253:             "vanilla_html" | "vanilla" | "html" => RuntimeType::VanillaHtml,
254:             "rust_backend" | "rust" => RuntimeType::RustBackend,
255:             "node_express" | "express" => RuntimeType::NodeExpress,
256:             "node_fastify" | "fastify" => RuntimeType::NodeFastify,
257:             "node_nest" | "nest" => RuntimeType::NodeNest,
258:             "python_fastapi" | "fastapi" => RuntimeType::PythonFastapi,
259:             "python_flask" | "flask" => RuntimeType::PythonFlask,
260:             "python_django" | "django" => RuntimeType::PythonDjango,
261:             "fullstack_react_rust" => RuntimeType::FullstackReactRust,
262:             "fullstack_react_node" => RuntimeType::FullstackReactNode,
263:             "fullstack_vanilla_rust" => RuntimeType::FullstackVanillaRust,
264:             "fullstack_vanilla_node" => RuntimeType::FullstackVanillaNode,
265:             "tauri_react" => RuntimeType::TauriReact,
266:             "tauri" => RuntimeType::TauriVanilla,
267:             "electron_react" | "electron" => RuntimeType::ElectronReact,
268:             "node_tool" => RuntimeType::NodeTool,
269:             "rust_cli" => RuntimeType::RustCli,
270:             _ => RuntimeType::Unknown,
271:         };
272: 
273:         // Parse frontend config
274:         let frontend = json.get("frontend").and_then(|f| {
275:             Some(FrontendRuntime {
276:                 framework: match f
277:                     .get("framework")
278:                     .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
279:                     .unwrap_or("vanilla")
280:                 {
281:                     "react" => FrontendFramework::React,
282:                     "vue" => FrontendFramework::Vue,
283:                     "solid" => FrontendFramework::Solid,
284:                     "svelte" => FrontendFramework::Svelte,
285:                     _ => FrontendFramework::Vanilla,
286:                 },
287:                 dev_port: f.get("dev_port").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()).unwrap_or(5173) as u16,
288:                 dev_host: f
289:                     .get("dev_host")
290:                     .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
291:                     .unwrap_or("localhost")
292:                     .to_string(),
293:                 dev_command: f
294:                     .get("dev_command")
295:                     .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
296:                     .unwrap_or("npm run dev")
297:                     .to_string(),
298:                 build_command: f
299:                     .get("build_command")
300:                     .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
301:                     .unwrap_or("npm run build")
302:                     .to_string(),
303:                 output_dir: f
304:                     .get("output_dir")
305:                     .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
306:                     .unwrap_or("dist")
307:                     .to_string(),
308:                 has_hmr: f.get("has_hmr").and_then(|v| v.as_bool()).unwrap_or(true),
309:                 entry_file: f
310:                     .get("entry_file")
311:                     .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
312:                     .unwrap_or("index.html")
313:                     .to_string(),
314:                 proxy_config: f.get("proxy_config").and_then(|p| {
315:                     Some(ProxyConfig {
316:                         enabled: p.get("enabled").and_then(|v| v.as_bool()).unwrap_or(false),
317:                         target: p
318:                             .get("target")
319:                             .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
320:                             .unwrap_or("")
321:                             .to_string(),
322:                         change_origin: p
323:                             .get("change_origin")
324:                             .and_then(|v| v.as_bool())
325:                             .unwrap_or(true),
326:                         ws: p.get("ws").and_then(|v| v.as_bool()).unwrap_or(true),
327:                     })
328:                 }),
329:             })
330:         });
331: 
332:         // Parse backend config
333:         let backend = json.get("backend").and_then(|b| {
334:             Some(BackendRuntime {
335:                 framework: match b
336:                     .get("framework")
337:                     .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
338:                     .unwrap_or("express")
339:                 {
340:                     "axum" => BackendFramework::Axum,
341:                     "actix_web" | "actix" => BackendFramework::ActixWeb,
342:                     "warp" => BackendFramework::Warp,
343:                     "rocket" => BackendFramework::Rocket,
344:                     "express" => BackendFramework::Express,
345:                     "fastify" => BackendFramework::Fastify,
346:                     "nest" => BackendFramework::Nest,
347:                     "fastapi" => BackendFramework::FastApi,
348:                     "flask" => BackendFramework::Flask,
349:                     "django" => BackendFramework::Django,
350:                     _ => BackendFramework::Express,
351:                 },
352:                 port: b.get("port").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()).unwrap_or(3000) as u16,
353:                 host: b
354:                     .get("host")
355:                     .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
356:                     .unwrap_or("0.0.0.0")
357:                     .to_string(),
358:                 dev_command: b
359:                     .get("dev_command")
360:                     .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
361:                     .unwrap_or("cargo run")
362:                     .to_string(),
363:                 build_command: b
364:                     .get("build_command")
365:                     .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
366:                     .unwrap_or("")
367:                     .to_string(),
368:                 start_command: b
369:                     .get("start_command")
370:                     .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
371:                     .map(String::from),
372:                 binary_name: b
373:                     .get("binary_name")
374:                     .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
375:                     .map(String::from),
376:                 entry_file: b
377:                     .get("entry_file")
378:                     .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
379:                     .unwrap_or("src/main.rs")
380:                     .to_string(),
381:                 api_base: b
382:                     .get("api_base")
383:                     .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
384:                     .unwrap_or("/api")
385:                     .to_string(),
386:             })
387:         });
388: 
389:         // Parse fullstack config
390:         let fullstack = json.get("fullstack").and_then(|fs| {
391:             Some(FullstackRuntime {
392:                 frontend_dev_command: fs
393:                     .get("frontend_dev_command")
394:                     .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
395:                     .unwrap_or("npm run dev")
396:                     .to_string(),
397:                 backend_dev_command: fs
398:                     .get("backend_dev_command")
399:                     .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
400:                     .unwrap_or("cargo run")
401:                     .to_string(),
402:                 frontend_port: fs
403:                     .get("frontend_port")
404:                     .and_then(|v| v.as_u64())
405:                     .unwrap_or(5173) as u16,
406:                 backend_port: fs
407:                     .get("backend_port")
408:                     .and_then(|v| v.as_u64())
409:                     .unwrap_or(3000) as u16,
410:                 concurrent: fs
411:                     .get("concurrent")
412:                     .and_then(|v| v.as_bool())
413:                     .unwrap_or(true),
414:                 proxy: fs.get("proxy").and_then(|p| {
415:                     Some(ProxyConfig {
416:                         enabled: p.get("enabled").and_then(|v| v.as_bool()).unwrap_or(true),
417:                         target: p
418:                             .get("target")
419:                             .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
420:                             .unwrap_or("http:
421:                             .to_string(),
422:                         change_origin: p
423:                             .get("change_origin")
424:                             .and_then(|v| v.as_bool())
425:                             .unwrap_or(true),
426:                         ws: p.get("ws").and_then(|v| v.as_bool()).unwrap_or(true),
427:                     })
428:                 }),
429:             })
430:         });
431: 
432:         
433:         let dependencies = json
434:             .get("dependencies")
435:             .and_then(|d| {
436:                 Some(DependencyConfig {
437:                     install_command: d
438:                         .get("install_command")
439:                         .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
440:                         .unwrap_or("npm install")
441:                         .to_string(),
442:                     package_manager: match d
443:                         .get("package_manager")
444:                         .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
445:                         .unwrap_or("npm")
446:                     {
447:                         "bun" => PackageManager::Bun,
448:                         "yarn" => PackageManager::Yarn,
449:                         "pnpm" => PackageManager::Pnpm,
450:                         "cargo" => PackageManager::Cargo,
451:                         "pip" => PackageManager::Pip,
452:                         "uv" => PackageManager::Uv,
453:                         _ => PackageManager::Npm,
454:                     },
455:                     install_timeout_secs: d
456:                         .get("install_timeout_secs")
457:                         .and_then(|v| v.as_u64())
458:                         .unwrap_or(300),
459:                 })
460:             })
461:             .unwrap_or_default();
462: 
463:         Ok(ProjectRuntimeConfig {
464:             runtime_type,
465:             frontend,
466:             backend,
467:             fullstack,
468:             dependencies,
469:             project_root: None,
470:             security_warnings: None,
471:             analyzed_at: chrono::Utc::now().to_rfc3339(),
472:             raw_analysis: None,
473:         })
474:     }
475: 
476:     
477:     fn analyze_with_heuristic(
478:         &self,
479:         info: &ProjectInfo,
480:         workspace: &Path,
481:     ) -> Result<ProjectRuntimeConfig, String> {
482:         let has_package_json = info.package_json.is_some();
483:         let has_cargo_toml = info.cargo_toml.is_some();
484: 
485:         
486:         let has_tauri = info
487:             .detected_files
488:             .iter()
489:             .any(|f| f.contains("src-tauri/tauri.conf.json"));
490: 
491:         
492:         let runtime_type = if has_package_json && has_cargo_toml {
493:             
494:             if let Some(pkg) = &info.package_json {
495:                 let deps = pkg.get("dependencies").or(pkg.get("devDependencies"));
496:                 if deps.and_then(|d| d.get("react")).is_some() {
497:                     RuntimeType::FullstackReactRust
498:                 } else {
499:                     RuntimeType::FullstackVanillaRust
500:                 }
501:             } else {
502:                 RuntimeType::FullstackReactRust
503:             }
504:         } else if has_package_json {
505:             
506:             if let Some(pkg) = &info.package_json {
507:                 let deps = pkg.get("dependencies").or(pkg.get("devDependencies"));
508: 
509:                 if has_tauri || info.detected_files.iter().any(|f| f.contains("src-tauri")) {
510:                     RuntimeType::TauriReact
511:                 } else if deps.and_then(|d| d.get("react")).is_some() {
512:                     RuntimeType::ReactVite
513:                 } else if deps.and_then(|d| d.get("vue")).is_some() {
514:                     RuntimeType::VueVite
515:                 } else if deps.and_then(|d| d.get("@nestjs/core")).is_some() {
516:                     RuntimeType::NodeNest
517:                 } else if deps.and_then(|d| d.get("express")).is_some() {
518:                     RuntimeType::NodeExpress
519:                 } else if deps.and_then(|d| d.get("fastify")).is_some() {
520:                     RuntimeType::NodeFastify
521:                 } else {
522:                     RuntimeType::NodeTool
523:                 }
524:             } else {
525:                 RuntimeType::NodeTool
526:             }
527:         } else if has_cargo_toml {
528:             
529:             if has_tauri {
530:                 RuntimeType::TauriVanilla
531:             } else if let Some(toml) = &info.cargo_toml {
532:                 if toml.contains("[[bin]]") || toml.contains("[[lib]]") {
533:                     
534:                     if toml.contains("axum")
535:                         || toml.contains("actix-web")
536:                         || toml.contains("warp")
537:                         || toml.contains("rocket")
538:                     {
539:                         RuntimeType::RustBackend
540:                     } else {
541:                         RuntimeType::RustCli
542:                     }
543:                 } else {
544:                     RuntimeType::RustCli
545:                 }
546:             } else {
547:                 RuntimeType::RustCli
548:             }
549:         } else if info
550:             .detected_files
551:             .iter()
552:             .any(|f| f.ends_with("index.html"))
553:         {
554:             RuntimeType::VanillaHtml
555:         } else if info
556:             .detected_files
557:             .iter()
558:             .any(|f| f.ends_with("main.py") || f.ends_with("app.py"))
559:         {
560:             
561:             if let Some(_pkg_json) = &info.package_json {
562:                 
563:                 RuntimeType::NodeTool
564:             } else {
565:                 RuntimeType::PythonFastapi 
566:             }
567:         } else {
568:             RuntimeType::Unknown
569:         };
570: 
571:         
572:         let mut config =
573:             crate::project_runtime::get_preset_config(&runtime_type).unwrap_or_else(|| {
574:                 ProjectRuntimeConfig {
575:                     runtime_type: RuntimeType::Unknown,
576:                     ..Default::default()
577:                 }
578:             });
579: 
580:         config.project_root = Some(workspace.to_path_buf());
581: 
582:         
583:         let checker = RuntimeSecurityChecker::new().with_project_root(workspace.to_path_buf());
584:         let result = checker.check_config(&config);
585: 
586:         if !result.is_safe {
587:             return Err(format!("安全检查失败: {}", result.errors.join("; ")));
588:         }
589: 
590:         if !result.warnings.is_empty() {
591:             config.security_warnings = Some(result.warnings);
592:         }
593: 
594:         Ok(config)
595:     }
596: }
597: ⋮----
598: RuntimeAnalyzer
599: ⋮----
600: {
601:     fn default() -> Self {
602:         Self::new()
603:     }
604: }
605: ⋮----
606: save_runtime_config
607: ⋮----
608: (workspace: &Path, config: &ProjectRuntimeConfig)
609: ⋮----
610: load_runtime_config
611: ⋮----
612: (workspace: &Path)
613: ⋮----
614: has_runtime_config
615: ⋮----
616: (workspace: &Path)
617: ⋮----
618: analyze_runtime_from_readme
619: ⋮----
620: (
621:     workspace: &Path,
622:     _config: &ProjectRuntimeConfig,
623:     llm_config: &LlmConfig,
624: )

### crates/cowork-gui/README.md (88 lines)

````
1: # 🖥️ Cowork Forge GUI
2: 
3: <p align="center">
4:   <strong>Your Personal AI Development Team - Visualized</strong>
5: </p>
6: 
7: <p align="center">
8:   <a href="../../README.md">Core Repository</a> | <a href="../../README_zh.md">中文文档</a>
9: </p>
10: 
11: Cowork Forge GUI is the visual command center for your AI development team. Built with **Tauri v2** and **React**, it transforms the abstract process of AI code generation into a tangible, interactive, and manageable workflow.
12: 
13: Instead of staring at a terminal, watch your **Product Manager**, **Architect**, and **Engineer** agents collaborate in real-time to build your software.
14: 
15: ---
16: 
17: ## ✨ Key Features & Visual Walkthrough
18: 
19: Experience the full lifecycle of software development through our intuitive interface.
20: 
21: |  |  |
22: | :--- | :--- |
23: | **📄 Live Artifact Preview**：Watch documents being written in real-time. View formatted **PRDs**, **System Designs**, and **Implementation Plans** as they are generated. Support for Markdown rendering allows for clear, structured documentation reviews. ![Artifact Viewer](../../assets/snapshots/artifact_preview.png) | **💬 Interactive Agent Chat**：Collaborate directly with your AI team. Discuss requirements with the Product Manager, review architecture with the Architect, or give feedback to the Engineer. The chat interface keeps the context of your entire project history. ![Agent Chat Interface](../../assets/snapshots/chat_preview.png) |
24: | **🌐 Built-in App Preview**：See the result instantly. Cowork Forge GUI includes a web view to preview your generated web applications immediately after the build completes, without leaving the tool. ![App Preview](../../assets/snapshots/app_preview.png) | **📝 Task & Todo Management**：Keep track of progress. The system automatically generates and maintains a Todo list for the current iteration, checking off items as the agents complete them. ![Todo List](../../assets/snapshots/todo_preview.png) |
25: | **🚀 Project Dashboard**：The central hub for all your development initiatives. View active projects, check their status (Running, Paused, Completed), and quickly launch new ones from a clean, modern interface.![Project Dashboard](../../assets/snapshots/dashboard_preview.png) | **⚡ Real-time Code Execution**：Monitor the **Coding Agent** as it writes files, runs builds, and executes tests. The built-in terminal view shows you exactly what commands are being run and their output, ensuring transparency and control. ![Terminal & Execution](../../assets/snapshots/execution_preview.png) |
26: 
27: ---
28: 
29: ## 🔄 Development Workflow
30: 
31: Cowork Forge GUI guides you through the standard **7-Stage Development Lifecycle**:
32: 
33: 1.  **💡 Idea Intake**: Chat with the agent to define your concept.
34: 2.  **📋 PRD Generation**: Review the generated Product Requirements Document.
35: 3.  **🏗️ Architecture Design**: Approve the technical design and system boundaries.
36: 4.  **📅 Implementation Planning**: See the task breakdown and timeline.
37: 5.  **💻 Coding & Testing**: Watch code being written and tests passing.
38: 6.  **✅ Quality Check**: Verify the implementation against requirements.
39: 7.  **📦 Delivery**: Receive the final project report and artifacts.
40: 
41: ---
42: 
43: ## 🛠️ Technical Architecture
44: 
45: The GUI is built on a robust, high-performance stack designed for local-first AI development:
46: 
47: *   **Frontend**: **React** + **Vite** + **Ant Design** for a responsive and familiar UX.
48: *   **Backend**: **Rust** (via **Tauri v2**) for native performance, system access, and security.
49: *   **Core Integration**: Directly embeds `cowork-core` to run the AI pipeline locally without external dependencies (except LLM APIs).
50: *   **State Management**: Real-time event streaming connects the Rust core logic with the React frontend.
51: 
52: ---
53: 
54: ## 🚀 Getting Started
55: 
56: ### Prerequisites
57: 
58: *   **Rust** (Stable or Nightly)
59: *   **Node.js** (or Bun/pnpm)
60: *   **System Dependencies** for Tauri (Linux/macOS/Windows specific libs)
61: 
62: ### Installation & Run
63: 
64: ```bash
65: # Navigate to the GUI directory
66: cd crates/cowork-gui
67: 
68: # Install frontend dependencies
69: npm install  # or 'bun install'
70: 
71: # Run in development mode (with hot reload)
72: npm run tauri:dev  # or 'cargo tauri dev'
73: ```
74: 
75: ### Building for Production
76: 
77: ```bash
78: # Build a standalone executable/installer
79: npm run tauri:build # or 'cargo tauri build'
80: ```
81: 
82: The output binary will be located in `src-tauri/target/release/bundle/`.
83: 
84: ---
85: 
86: <p align="center">
87:   Built with ❤️ by the Cowork Forge Team
88: </p>
````

### crates/cowork-gui/src/hooks/useAutoScroll.ts (23 lines)

```
1: function useAutoScroll<T extends HTMLElement>(deps: unknown[] = []) {
2:   const ref = useRef<T>(null);
3:   
4:   const scrollToBottom = useCallback(() => {
5:     if (ref.current) {
6:       ref.current.scrollTop = ref.current.scrollHeight;
7:     }
8:   }, []);
9:   
10:   return { ref, scrollToBottom };
11: }
12: ⋮----
13: function useAutoScrollOnDeps<T extends HTMLElement>(deps: unknown[] = []) {
14:   const ref = useRef<T>(null);
15:   
16:   const scrollToBottom = useCallback(() => {
17:     if (ref.current) {
18:       ref.current.scrollTop = ref.current.scrollHeight;
19:     }
20:   }, []);
21:   
22:   return { ref };
23: }
```

### crates/cowork-gui/src/hooks/useChatInput.ts (156 lines)

```
1: function useChatInput() {
2: 	
3: 	const { updateCurrentIterationStatus } = useProjectStore();
4: 
5: 	
6: 	const {
7: 		inputRequest,
8: 		addMessage,
9: 		setMessages,
10: 		setInputRequest,
11: 		submitInput
12: 	} = useAgentStore();
13: 
14: 	
15: 	const {
16: 		setActiveView,
17: 		setActiveArtifactTab,
18: 		triggerCodeRefresh,
19: 		triggerArtifactsRefresh
20: 	} = useUIStore();
21: 
22: 	
23: 	const handleSendUserMessage = useCallback(
24: 		async (userInput: string, setUserInput: (input: string) => void) => {
25: 			if (!userInput.trim()) return;
26: 			const msgContent = userInput;
27: 			addMessage({
28: 				type: 'user',
29: 				content: msgContent,
30: 				timestamp: new Date().toISOString()
31: 			} as ChatMessage);
32: 
33: 			if (inputRequest) {
34: 				await submitInput(msgContent, 'text');
35: 			}
36: 			setUserInput('');
37: 		},
38: 		[inputRequest, addMessage, submitInput]
39: 	);
40: 
41: 	
42: 	const handleSelectOption = useCallback(
43: 		async (option: InputOption, userInput: string, setUserInput: (input: string) => void) => {
44: 			if (!inputRequest) return;
45: 
46: 			if (option.id === 'view_artifact' && inputRequest.isArtifactConfirmation) {
47: 				const artifactTypeToTab: Record<string, string> = {
48: 					idea: 'idea',
49: 					requirements: 'requirements',
50: 					design: 'design',
51: 					plan: 'plan',
52: 					code: 'code'
53: 				};
54: 				const targetTab = artifactTypeToTab[inputRequest.artifactType || ''] || 'idea';
55: 				setActiveArtifactTab(targetTab);
56: 
57: 				if (inputRequest.artifactType === 'code') {
58: 					setActiveView('code');
59: 					triggerCodeRefresh();
60: 				} else {
61: 					setActiveView('artifacts');
62: 					triggerArtifactsRefresh();
63: 				}
64: 				return;
65: 			}
66: 
67: 			if (option.id === 'feedback' && inputRequest.isArtifactConfirmation) {
68: 				setInputRequest({
69: 					...inputRequest,
70: 					isFeedbackMode: true,
71: 					feedbackPrompt: 'Please enter your feedback:'
72: 				});
73: 				setUserInput('');
74: 				return;
75: 			}
76: 
77: 			addMessage({
78: 				type: 'user',
79: 				content: option.label,
80: 				timestamp: new Date().toISOString()
81: 			} as ChatMessage);
82: 			await submitInput(option.id, 'selection');
83: 
84: 			
85: 			if (option.id === 'yes') {
86: 				updateCurrentIterationStatus('Running');
87: 			}
88: 
89: 			setUserInput('');
90: 		},
91: 		[
92: 			inputRequest,
93: 			addMessage,
94: 			submitInput,
95: 			setActiveView,
96: 			setActiveArtifactTab,
97: 			triggerCodeRefresh,
98: 			triggerArtifactsRefresh,
99: 			setInputRequest,
100: 			updateCurrentIterationStatus
101: 		]
102: 	);
103: 
104: 	
105: 	const handleSubmitFeedback = useCallback(
106: 		async (userInput: string, setUserInput: (input: string) => void, updateCurrentIterationStatus: (status: string) => void) => {
107: 			if (!inputRequest || !userInput.trim()) return;
108: 			const feedback = userInput.trim();
109: 			addMessage({
110: 				type: 'agent',
111: 				content: '📝 Feedback received. Regenerating...',
112: 				agentName: 'System',
113: 				timestamp: new Date().toISOString()
114: 			} as ChatMessage);
115: 			addMessage({
116: 				type: 'user',
117: 				content: `💬 Feedback:\n${feedback}`,
118: 				timestamp: new Date().toISOString()
119: 			} as ChatMessage);
120: 			await submitInput(feedback, 'text');
121: 			updateCurrentIterationStatus('Running');
122: 			setUserInput('');
123: 		},
124: 		[inputRequest, addMessage, submitInput]
125: 	);
126: 
127: 	
128: 	const handleToggleThinking = useCallback(
129: 		(index: number) => {
130: 			setMessages((prev) =>
131: 				prev.map((m, i) =>
132: 					i === index && m.type === 'thinking'
133: 						? ({ ...m, isExpanded: !(m as ThinkingMessage).isExpanded } as ChatMessage)
134: 						: m
135: 				)
136: 			);
137: 		},
138: 		[setMessages]
139: 	);
140: 
141: 	
142: 	const handleCancelFeedback = useCallback(() => {
143: 		if (inputRequest) {
144: 			setInputRequest({ ...inputRequest, isFeedbackMode: false });
145: 		}
146: 	}, [inputRequest, setInputRequest]);
147: 
148: 	return {
149: 		inputRequest,
150: 		handleSendUserMessage,
151: 		handleSelectOption,
152: 		handleSubmitFeedback,
153: 		handleToggleThinking,
154: 		handleCancelFeedback
155: 	};
156: }
```

### crates/cowork-gui/src/hooks/useIterationEvents.ts (71 lines)

```
1: UseIterationEventsOptions
2: ⋮----
3: {
4:   onIterationCreated?: () => void;
5:   onIterationStarted?: () => void;
6:   onIterationContinued?: () => void;
7:   onIterationCompleted?: () => void;
8:   onIterationFailed?: () => void;
9:   onAgentEvent?: (content: string) => void;
10:   onExecutingIdChange?: (id: string | null) => void;
11: }
12: ⋮----
13: function useIterationEvents(options: UseIterationEventsOptions = {}) {
14:   const {
15:     onIterationCreated,
16:     onIterationStarted,
17:     onIterationContinued,
18:     onIterationCompleted,
19:     onIterationFailed,
20:     onAgentEvent,
21:     onExecutingIdChange,
22:   } = options;
23: 
24:   useEffect(() => {
25:     const unlistenCreated = listen(TAURI_EVENTS.ITERATION_CREATED, () => {
26:       onIterationCreated?.();
27:     });
28: 
29:     const unlistenStarted = listen(TAURI_EVENTS.ITERATION_STARTED, () => {
30:       setTimeout(() => onIterationStarted?.(), 500);
31:     });
32: 
33:     const unlistenContinued = listen(TAURI_EVENTS.ITERATION_CONTINUED, () => {
34:       setTimeout(() => onIterationContinued?.(), 500);
35:     });
36: 
37:     const unlistenAgentEvent = listen(TAURI_EVENTS.AGENT_EVENT, (event) => {
38:       const content = (event.payload as { content?: string })?.content || "";
39:       if (content.includes("Starting stage:") && onAgentEvent) {
40:         setTimeout(() => onAgentEvent(content), 100);
41:       }
42:     });
43: 
44:     const unlistenCompleted = listen(TAURI_EVENTS.ITERATION_COMPLETED, () => {
45:       onIterationCompleted?.();
46:       onExecutingIdChange?.(null);
47:     });
48: 
49:     const unlistenFailed = listen(TAURI_EVENTS.ITERATION_FAILED, () => {
50:       onIterationFailed?.();
51:       onExecutingIdChange?.(null);
52:     });
53: 
54:     return () => {
55:       unlistenCreated.then((fn) => fn()).catch(() => {});
56:       unlistenStarted.then((fn) => fn()).catch(() => {});
57:       unlistenContinued.then((fn) => fn()).catch(() => {});
58:       unlistenAgentEvent.then((fn) => fn()).catch(() => {});
59:       unlistenCompleted.then((fn) => fn()).catch(() => {});
60:       unlistenFailed.then((fn) => fn()).catch(() => {});
61:     };
62:   }, [
63:     onIterationCreated,
64:     onIterationStarted,
65:     onIterationContinued,
66:     onIterationCompleted,
67:     onIterationFailed,
68:     onAgentEvent,
69:     onExecutingIdChange,
70:   ]);
71: }
```

### crates/cowork-gui/src/hooks/useIterationsData.ts (62 lines)

```
1: UseIterationsDataResult
2: ⋮----
3: {
4:   iterations: IterationInfo[];
5:   project: ProjectInfo | null;
6:   loading: boolean;
7:   executingId: string | null;
8:   setExecutingId: (id: string | null) => void;
9:   loadData: () => Promise<void>;
10: }
11: ⋮----
12: function useIterationsData(): UseIterationsDataResult {
13:   const { message } = App.useApp();
14:   const [iterations, setIterations] = useState<IterationInfo[]>([]);
15:   const [project, setProject] = useState<ProjectInfo | null>(null);
16:   const [loading, setLoading] = useState(false);
17:   const [executingId, setExecutingId] = useState<string | null>(null);
18: 
19:   const loadData = async () => {
20:     setLoading(true);
21:     try {
22:       const projectData = await invoke<ProjectInfo | null>("gui_get_project");
23:       setProject(projectData);
24: 
25:       const iterationsData = await invoke<IterationInfo[]>("gui_get_iterations");
26:       const sortedIterations = (iterationsData || []).sort((a, b) => {
27:         return new Date(b.created_at).getTime() - new Date(a.created_at).getTime();
28:       });
29:       setIterations(sortedIterations);
30:     } catch (error) {
31:       console.error("[useIterationsData] Failed to load data:", error);
32:       message.error("Failed to load data: " + error);
33:     } finally {
34:       setLoading(false);
35:     }
36:   };
37: 
38:   
39:   useEffect(() => {
40:     loadData();
41:   }, []);
42: 
43:   
44:   useIterationEvents({
45:     onIterationCreated: loadData,
46:     onIterationStarted: loadData,
47:     onIterationContinued: loadData,
48:     onIterationCompleted: loadData,
49:     onIterationFailed: loadData,
50:     onAgentEvent: loadData,
51:     onExecutingIdChange: setExecutingId,
52:   });
53: 
54:   return {
55:     iterations,
56:     project,
57:     loading,
58:     executingId,
59:     setExecutingId,
60:     loadData
61:   };
62: }
```

### crates/cowork-gui/src/hooks/useLoading.ts (48 lines)

```
1: function useLoading<T>(
2:   asyncFn: (...args: unknown[]) => Promise<T>
3: ): [typeof asyncFn, boolean, Error | null] {
4:   const [loading, setLoading] = useState(false);
5:   const [error, setError] = useState<Error | null>(null);
6:   
7:   const wrappedFn = useCallback(
8:     async (...args: Parameters<typeof asyncFn>) => {
9:       setLoading(true);
10:       setError(null);
11:       try {
12:         const result = await asyncFn(...args);
13:         setLoading(false);
14:         return result;
15:       } catch (e) {
16:         setError(e instanceof Error ? e : new Error(String(e)));
17:         setLoading(false);
18:         throw e;
19:       }
20:     },
21:     [asyncFn]
22:   );
23:   
24:   return [wrappedFn, loading, error];
25: }
26: ⋮----
27: function useAsyncAction<T>(
28:   asyncFn: () => Promise<T>,
29:   deps: unknown[] = []
30: ): [() => Promise<void>, boolean, Error | null] {
31:   const [loading, setLoading] = useState(false);
32:   const [error, setError] = useState<Error | null>(null);
33:   
34:   const execute = useCallback(async () => {
35:     setLoading(true);
36:     setError(null);
37:     try {
38:       await asyncFn();
39:     } catch (e) {
40:       setError(e instanceof Error ? e : new Error(String(e)));
41:       throw e;
42:     } finally {
43:       setLoading(false);
44:     }
45:   }, deps);
46:   
47:   return [execute, loading, error];
48: }
```

### crates/cowork-gui/src/hooks/useModal.ts (18 lines)

1: function useModal(): [boolean, () => void, () => void, () => void] {
2:   const [visible, setVisible] = useState(false);
3:   
4:   const open = useCallback(() => setVisible(true), []);
5:   const close = useCallback(() => setVisible(false), []);
6:   const toggle = useCallback(() => setVisible((v) => !v), []);
7:   
8:   return [visible, open, close, toggle];
9: }
10: ⋮----
11: function useDrawer(): [boolean, () => void, () => void] {
12:   const [visible, setVisible] = useState(false);
13:   
14:   const open = useCallback(() => setVisible(true), []);
15:   const close = useCallback(() => setVisible(false), []);
16:   
17:   return [visible, open, close];
18: }

### crates/cowork-gui/src/hooks/usePMAgent.ts (192 lines)

```
1: function usePMAgent() {
2: 	const { message } = AntApp.useApp();
3: 
4: 	
5: 	const { currentIteration, loadProject, setCurrentIteration } = useProjectStore();
6: 
7: 	
8: 	const { setMessages, clearPMMessages, setPmProcessing, sendPMMessage } = useAgentStore();
9: 
10: 	
11: 	const { setActiveView, setActiveArtifactTab } = useUIStore();
12: 
13: 	
14: 	const buildPMFeedback = useCallback(
15: 		(msgs: (ChatMessage & { type: 'user' | 'pm_agent' })[], targetStage: string): string => {
16: 			console.log(
17: 				'[PM] buildPMFeedback called with',
18: 				msgs.length,
19: 				'messages, targetStage:',
20: 				targetStage
21: 			);
22: 
23: 			const userMessages = msgs
24: 				.filter((msg) => msg.type === 'user')
25: 				.map((msg) => (msg as { content: string }).content)
26: 				.filter((content) => content && content.trim());
27: 
28: 			if (userMessages.length === 0) return '';
29: 
30: 			const result = userMessages.join('\n\n');
31: 			console.log('[PM] buildPMFeedback result length:', result.length);
32: 			return result;
33: 		},
34: 		[]
35: 	);
36: 
37: 	
38: 	const handlePMAction = useCallback(
39: 		async (action: PMAction, pmMessages: (ChatMessage & { type: 'user' | 'pm_agent' })[]) => {
40: 			if (!currentIteration) return;
41: 
42: 			switch (action.action_type) {
43: 				case 'pm_start_app':
44: 					setActiveView('run');
45: 					message.info('Starting application...');
46: 					try {
47: 						const isRunning = await invoke<boolean>('check_project_status', {
48: 							iterationId: currentIteration.id
49: 						});
50: 						if (isRunning) {
51: 							message.info('Application is already running');
52: 							return;
53: 						}
54: 					} catch {}
55: 
56: 					try {
57: 						await invoke('start_iteration_project', { iterationId: currentIteration.id });
58: 					} catch (err) {
59: 						message.error('Failed to start app: ' + err);
60: 					}
61: 					beak;
62: 
63: 				case 'pm_open_folder':
64: 					try {
65: 						await invoke('open_in_file_manager', { path: `workspace_${currentIteration.id}` });
66: 					} catch (err) {
67: 						message.error('Failed to open folder: ' + err);
68: 					}
69: 					beak;
70: 
71: 				case 'pm_view_knowledge':
72: 					setActiveView('project-knowledge');
73: 					beak;
74: 
75: 				case 'pm_view_artifacts':
76: 					setActiveView('artifacts');
77: 					setActiveArtifactTab('design');
78: 					beak;
79: 
80: 				case 'pm_view_code':
81: 					setActiveView('code');
82: 					beak;
83: 
84: 				case 'pm_goto_stage':
85: 					if (action.target_stage) {
86: 						console.log('[PM] pm_goto_stage action received, target_stage:', action.target_stage);
87: 						const feedbackText = buildPMFeedback(pmMessages, action.target_stage);
88: 						console.log(
89: 							'[PM] buildPMFeedback result:',
90: 							feedbackText
91: 								? `${feedbackText.length} chars: ${feedbackText.substring(0, 100)}...`
92: 								: 'empty'
93: 						);
94: 						Modal.confirm({
95: 							title: 'Confirm Stage Return',
96: 							content: `Return to ${action.target_stage} stage? Your PM Chat conversation will be passed as feedback to the agent.`,
97: 							onOk: async () => {
98: 								try {
99: 									console.log(
100: 										'[PM] User confirmed, calling API.pm.restart with feedback:',
101: 										feedbackText ? `${feedbackText.length} chars` : 'none'
102: 									);
103: 									clearPMMessages();
104: 									await API.pm.restart(
105: 										currentIteration.id,
106: 										action.target_stage!,
107: 										feedbackText.length > 0 ? feedbackText : undefined
108: 									);
109: 									message.success(`Restarted from ${action.target_stage}`);
110: 									loadProject();
111: 								} catch (err) {
112: 									message.error('Failed: ' + err);
113: 								}
114: 							}
115: 						});
116: 					}
117: 					beak;
118: 
119: 				case 'pm_create_iteration':
120: 					if (action.iteration_id) {
121: 						Modal.confirm({
122: 							title: '启动新迭代',
123: 							content: `是否启动新迭代「${action.title || 'Untitled'}」？`,
124: 							onOk: async () => {
125: 								try {
126: 									clearPMMessages();
127: 									setMessages([]);
128: 									await loadProject();
129: 									const newIteration = await API.iteration.get(action.iteration_id!);
130: 									setCurrentIteration(newIteration);
131: 									await API.iteration.execute(action.iteration_id!);
132: 									message.success('新迭代已启动');
133: 									setActiveView('chat');
134: 								} catch (err) {
135: 									message.error('启动失败: ' + err);
136: 								}
137: 							}
138: 						});
139: 					}
140: 					beak;
141: 
142: 				default:
143: 					console.log('Unknown PM action:', action);
144: 			}
145: 		},
146: 		[
147: 			currentIteration,
148: 			loadProject,
149: 			setActiveView,
150: 			setActiveArtifactTab,
151: 			clearPMMessages,
152: 			setMessages,
153: 			setCurrentIteration,
154: 			buildPMFeedback,
155: 			message
156: 		]
157: 	);
158: 
159: 	
160: 	const handlePMSendMessage = useCallback(
161: 		async (userInput: string, setUserInput: (input: string) => void) => {
162: 			console.log(
163: 				'[usePMAgent] handlePMSendMessage called, userInput:',
164: 				userInput,
165: 				'currentIteration:',
166: 				currentIteration?.id
167: 			);
168: 			if (!userInput.trim() || !currentIteration) {
169: 				console.log('[usePMAgent] handlePMSendMessage early return: no input or no iteration');
170: 				return;
171: 			}
172: 			const userMessage = userInput.trim();
173: 			setUserInput('');
174: 			setPmProcessing(true);
175: 
176: 			try {
177: 				await sendPMMessage(currentIteration.id, userMessage);
178: 			} catch (error) {
179: 				message.error('Failed to process message: ' + error);
180: 			} finally {
181: 				setPmProcessing(false);
182: 			}
183: 		},
184: 		[currentIteration, sendPMMessage, setPmProcessing, message]
185: 	);
186: 
187: 	return {
188: 		buildPMFeedback,
189: 		handlePMAction,
190: 		handlePMSendMessage
191: 	};
192: }
```

### crates/cowork-gui/src/hooks/useProjectEvents.ts (25 lines)

7: ⋮----
8: function useProjectEvents(options: UseProjectEventsOptions = {}) {
9:   const { onProjectLoaded, onProjectCreated } = options;
10: 
11:   useEffect(() => {
12:     const unlistenProjectLoaded = listen(TAURI_EVENTS.PROJECT_LOADED, () => {
13:       onProjectLoaded?.();
14:     });
15: 
16:     const unlistenProjectCreated = listen(TAURI_EVENTS.PROJECT_CREATED, () => {
17:       onProjectCreated?.();
18:     });
19: 
20:     return () => {
21:       unlistenProjectLoaded.then((fn) => fn()).catch(() => {});
22:       unlistenProjectCreated.then((fn) => fn()).catch(() => {});
23:     };
24:   }, [onProjectLoaded, onProjectCreated]);
25: }
```

### crates/cowork-gui/src/hooks/useRefreshTrigger.ts (21 lines)

```
1: function useRefreshTrigger(callback: () => void, trigger: number) {
2:   const prevTriggerRef = useRef(trigger);
3:   
4:   useEffect(() => {
5:     if (trigger !== prevTriggerRef.current) {
6:       prevTriggerRef.current = trigger;
7:       callback();
8:     }
9:   }, [trigger, callback]);
10: }
11: ⋮----
12: function useRefreshTriggerValue(initialValue = 0): [number, () => void] {
13:   const triggerRef = useRef(initialValue);
14:   
15:   const trigger = useCallback(() => {
16:     triggerRef.current += 1;
17:     return triggerRef.current;
18:   }, []);
19:   
20:   return [triggerRef.current, trigger];
21: }
```

### crates/cowork-gui/src/hooks/useTauriEvent.ts (42 lines)

```
1: function useTauriEvent<T = unknown>(
2:   event: string, 
3:   handler: (payload: T) => void,
4:   deps: unknown[] = []
5: ) {
6:   const handlerRef = useRef(handler);
7:   handlerRef.current = handler;
8:   
9:   useEffect(() => {
10:     let unlisten: UnlistenFn | undefined;
11:     
12:     const setup = async () => {
13:       unlisten = await listen<T>(event, (e) => {
14:         handlerRef.current(e.payload);
15:       });
16:     };
17:     
18:     setup();
19:     
20:     return () => {
21:       unlisten?.();
22:     };
23:   }, [event, ...deps]);
24: }
25: ⋮----
26: function useTauriEvents(
27:   events: Array<{ event: string; handler: (payload: unknown) => void }>
28: ) {
29:   useEffect(() => {
30:     const unlisteners: Promise<UnlistenFn>[] = [];
31:     
32:     events.forEach(({ event, handler }) => {
33:       unlisteners.push(listen(event, (e) => handler(e.payload)));
34:     });
35:     
36:     return () => {
37:       Promise.all(unlisteners).then((fns) => {
38:         fns.forEach((fn) => fn());
39:       });
40:     };
41:   }, []);
42: }
```

8:   algorithm: theme.defaultAlgorithm,
9:   token: {
10:     colorPrimary: '#2563eb',
11:     colorPrimaryHover: '#1d4ed8',

40:       itemSelectedBg: '#dbeafe',
41:       itemColor: '#64748b',
42:       itemSelectedColor: '#2563eb',
43:       itemHoverBg: '#f1f5f9',
44:       itemHoverColor: '#1e293b',
45:     },
46:     Button: {
47:       borderRadius: 8,
48:       boxShadow: '0 1px 2px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05)',
49:     },
50:     Input: {
51:       borderRadius: 10,
52:       activeShadow: '0 0 0 3px rgba(37, 99, 235, 0.1)',
53:     },
54:     Card: {
55:       borderRadius: 12,
56:       boxShadow: '0 1px 3px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05)',
57:     },
58:     Tabs: {
59:       cardBg: '#f8fafc',
60:       itemColor: '#64748b',
61:       itemSelectedColor: '#2563eb',
62:     },
63:     Tag: {
64:       borderRadius: 6,
65:     },
66:     Alert: {
67:       borderRadius: 10,
68:     },
69:     Modal: {
70:       borderRadius: 16,
71:     },
72:     Dropdown: {
73:       borderRadius: 12,
74:     },
75:   },
76: };
77: 
78: ReactDOM.createRoot(document.getElementById('root')!).render(
79:   <React.StrictMode>
80:     <ConfigProvider theme={lightTheme}>
81:       <AntApp>
82:         <App />
83:       </AntApp>
84:     </ConfigProvider>
85:   </React.StrictMode>
86: );
```

### crates/cowork-gui/src/styles/global.css (34 lines)

```
1: * {
2:   margin: 0;
3:   padding: 0;
4:   box-sizing: border-box;
5: }
6: 
7: body {
8:   font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, 'Helvetica Neue', Arial, sans-serif;
9:   background: var(--bg-base);
10:   color: var(--text-primary);
11:   height: 100vh;
12:   overflow: hidden;
13:   -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased;
14:   -moz-osx-font-smoothing: grayscale;
15: }
16: 
17: 
18: ::-webkit-scrollbar {
19:   width: 8px;
20:   height: 8px;
21: }
22: 
23: ::-webkit-scrollbar-track {
24:   background: transparent;
25: }
26: 
27: ::-webkit-scrollbar-thumb {
28:   background: var(--text-muted);
29:   border-radius: 4px;
30: }
31: 
32: ::-webkit-scrollbar-thumb:hover {
33:   background: var(--text-tertiary);
34: }
```

### crates/cowork-gui/src/styles/layout.css (131 lines)

```
1: .runner-panel,
2: .preview-panel,
3: .code-editor-container {
4:   background: var(--bg-base);
5: }
6: 
7: 
8: .app-header {
9:   background: var(--bg-base);
10:   border-bottom: 1px solid var(--border-color);
11:   padding: 0 24px;
12:   display: flex;
13:   align-items: center;
14:   justify-content: space-between;
15: }
16: 
17: .app-title {
18:   font-size: 18px;
19:   font-weight: 600;
20:   color: var(--text-primary);
21: }
22: 
23: 
24: .iterations-panel {
25:   background: var(--bg-sidebar);
26: }
27: 
28: 
29: .session-item {
30:   padding: 12px 16px;
31:   margin-bottom: 6px;
32:   border-radius: 10px;
33:   cursor: pointer;
34:   transition: all 0.2s ease;
35:   border: 1px solid transparent;
36: }
37: 
38: .session-item:hover {
39:   background: var(--bg-elevated);
40:   border-color: var(--border-color);
41: }
42: 
43: .session-item.active {
44:   background: var(--primary-light);
45:   border-color: var(--primary);
46: }
47: 
48: .session-item-title {
49:   color: var(--text-primary);
50:   font-size: 14px;
51:   font-weight: 500;
52: }
53: 
54: .session-item-time {
55:   color: var(--text-tertiary);
56:   font-size: 12px;
57:   margin-top: 4px;
58: }
59: 
60: 
61: .welcome-screen {
62:   display: flex;
63:   flex-direction: column;
64:   align-items: center;
65:   justify-content: center;
66:   height: 100%;
67:   color: var(--text-secondary);
68:   text-align: center;
69:   padding: 40px;
70: }
71: 
72: .welcome-screen h2 {
73:   color: var(--text-primary);
74:   font-size: 32px;
75:   margin-bottom: 16px;
76:   font-weight: 600;
77: }
78: 
79: .welcome-screen p {
80:   font-size: 16px;
81:   line-height: 1.6;
82:   max-width: 500px;
83:   margin: 0 auto;
84: }
85: 
86: 
87: .status-indicator {
88:   width: 8px;
89:   height: 8px;
90:   border-radius: 50%;
91:   display: inline-block;
92: }
93: 
94: .status-indicator.success {
95:   background: var(--success);
96:   box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--success-light);
97: }
98: 
99: .status-indicator.processing {
100:   background: var(--primary);
101:   box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--primary-light);
102:   animation: pulse 2s infinite;
103: }
104: 
105: .status-indicator.error {
106:   background: var(--error);
107:   box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--error-light);
108: }
109: 
110: 
111: @keyframes pulse {
112:   0%, 100% {
113:     opacity: 0.3;
114:     transform: scale(0.8);
115:   }
116:   50% {
117:     opacity: 1;
118:     transform: scale(1);
119:   }
120: }
121: 
122: .agent-loading-indicator {
123:   margin-left: 4px;
124: }
125: 
126: 
127: .divider {
128:   height: 1px;
129:   background: var(--border-color);
130:   margin: 16px 0;
131: }
```

### crates/cowork-gui/src/utils/errorHandler.ts (117 lines)

```
1: class AppError extends Error {
2:   constructor(
3:     message: string,
4:     public code?: string,
5:     public cause?: unknown
6:   ) {
7:     super(message);
8:     this.name = 'AppError';
9:   }
10: }
11: ⋮----
12: ErrorHandlerOptions
13: ⋮----
14: {
15:   showMessage?: boolean;
16:   fallback?: () => void;
17:   onError?: (error: AppError) => void;
18: }
19: ⋮----
20: function handleError(
21:   error: unknown,
22:   message: MessageInstance,
23:   context: string,
24:   options: ErrorHandlerOptions = {}
25: ): AppError {
26:   const { showMessage = true, fallback, onError } = options;
27: 
28:   let appError: AppError;
29: 
30:   if (error instanceof AppError) {
31:     appError = error;
32:   } else if (error instanceof Error) {
33:     appError = new AppError(error.message, undefined, error);
34:   } else if (typeof error === 'string') {
35:     appError = new AppError(error);
36:   } else {
37:     appError = new AppError('An unknown error occurred', undefined, error);
38:   }
39: 
40:   
41:   console.error(`[${context}]`, appError);
42: 
43:   
44:   if (showMessage) {
45:     message.error(`${context}: ${appError.message}`);
46:   }
47: 
48:   
49:   if (fallback) {
50:     fallback();
51:   }
52: 
53:   
54:   if (onError) {
55:     onError(appError);
56:   }
57: 
58:   return appError;
59: }
60: ⋮----
61: async function withErrorHandling<T>(
62:   fn: () => Promise<T>,
63:   message: MessageInstance,
64:   context: string,
65:   options: ErrorHandlerOptions = {}
66: ): Promise<T | null> {
67:   try {
68:     return await fn();
69:   } catch (error) {
70:     handleError(error, message, context, options);
71:     return null;
72:   }
73: }
74: ⋮----
75: function formatErrorMessage(error: unknown): string {
76:   if (error instanceof AppError) {
77:     return error.message;
78:   }
79:   if (error instanceof Error) {
80:     return error.message;
81:   }
82:   if (typeof error === 'string') {
83:     return error;
84:   }
85:   return 'An unknown error occurred';
86: }
87: ⋮----
88: function showSuccess(msg: string): void {
89:   globalMessage.success(msg);
90: }
91: ⋮----
92: function showError(error: unknown): void {
93:   const errorMsg = formatErrorMessage(error);
94:   globalMessage.error(errorMsg);
95: }
96: ⋮----
97: function showWarning(msg: string): void {
98:   globalMessage.warning(msg);
99: }
100: ⋮----
101: function showInfo(msg: string): void {
102:   globalMessage.info(msg);
103: }
104: ⋮----
105: async function tryExecute<T>(
106:   fn: () => Promise<T>,
107:   errorContext?: string
108: ): Promise<T | null> {
109:   try {
110:     return await fn();
111:   } catch (error) {
112:     const context = errorContext || 'Operation failed';
113:     showError(`${context}: ${formatErrorMessage(error)}`);
114:     console.error(`[${context}]`, error);
115:     return null;
116:   }
117: }
```

20:     }

62:         }
63:     }

65: ⋮----

70:     pub search: Option<String>,
71:     pub limit: Option<usize>,

73: ⋮----
74: ProjectRegistryManager
75: ⋮----
76: {
77:     registry_path: PathBuf,
78:     registry: ProjectRegistry,
79: }
80: ⋮----
81: ProjectRegistryManager
82: ⋮----
83: {
84:     
85:     pub fn new() -> Result<Self> {
86:         let registry_path = get_registry_path()?;
87: 
88:         
89:         if let Some(parent) = registry_path.parent() {
90:             fs::create_dir_all(parent).context("Failed to create registry directory")?;
91:         }
92: 
93:         
94:         let registry = if registry_path.exists() {
95:             Self::load_registry_from_file(&registry_path)?
96:         } else {
97:             ProjectRegistry::default()
98:         };
99: 
100:         Ok(Self {
101:             registry_path,
102:             registry,
103:         })
104:     }
105: 
106:     
107:     fn load_registry_from_file(path: &Path) -> Result<ProjectRegistry> {
108:         let content = fs::read_to_string(path).context("Failed to read registry file")?;
109: 
110:         
111:         let registry: ProjectRegistry = match serde_json::from_str(&content) {
112:             Ok(r) => r,
113:             Err(_) => {
114:                 
115:                 
116:                 let mut json: serde_json::Value =
117:                     serde_json::from_str(&content).context("Failed to parse registry as JSON")?;
118: 
119:                 
120:                 if let Some(projects) = json.get("projects").and_then(|p| p.as_array()) {
121:                     let mut migrated_projects = Vec::new();
122:                     for project in projects {
123:                         let mut project = project.clone();
124: 
125:                         
126:                         if let Some(metadata) = project.get_mut("metadata") {
127:                             if metadata.is_object() {
128:                                 let obj = metadata.as_object_mut().unwrap();
129: 
130:                                 
131:                                 if let Some(session_count) = obj.remove("session_count") {
132:                                     obj.insert("iteration_count".to_string(), session_count);
133:                                 }
134: 
135:                                 
136:                                 if let Some(last_session_id) = obj.remove("last_session_id") {
137:                                     obj.insert("last_iteration_id".to_string(), last_session_id);
138:                                 }
139:                             }
140:                         }
141: 
142:                         migrated_projects.push(project);
143:                     }
144: 
145:                     
146:                     if let Some(ref mut projects_json) = json.as_object_mut() {
147:                         projects_json.insert(
148:                             "projects".to_string(),
149:                             serde_json::Value::Array(migrated_projects),
150:                         );
151:                     }
152: 
153:                     
154:                     serde_json::from_value(json).context("Failed to parse migrated registry")?
155:                 } else {
156:                     anyhow::bail!("Failed to parse registry: no projects found")
157:                 }
158:             }
159:         };
160: 
161:         
162:         if registry.schema_version != SCHEMA_VERSION {
163:             anyhow::bail!(
164:                 "Registry schema version mismatch: expected {}, got {}",
165:                 SCHEMA_VERSION,
166:                 registry.schema_version
167:             );
168:         }
169: 
170:         Ok(registry)
171:     }
172: 
173:     
174:     fn save_registry_to_file(&self) -> Result<()> {
175:         let mut registry = self.registry.clone();
176:         registry.updated_at = Utc::now();
177: 
178:         let content =
179:             serde_json::to_string_pretty(&registry).context("Failed to serialize registry")?;
180: 
181:         fs::write(&self.registry_path, content).context("Failed to write registry file")?;
182: 
183:         Ok(())
184:     }
185: 
186:     
187:     pub fn register_project(
188:         &mut self,
189:         workspace_path: String,
190:         name: String,
191:         description: Option<String>,
192:     ) -> Result<ProjectRecord> {
193:         
194:         if self
195:             .registry
196:             .projects
197:             .iter()
198:             .any(|p| p.workspace_path == workspace_path)
199:         {
200:             anyhow::bail!("Project already registered: {}", workspace_path);
201:         }
202: 
203:         
204:         let workspace = Path::new(&workspace_path);
205:         if !workspace.exists() {
206:             
207:             fs::create_dir_all(workspace).context(format!(
208:                 "Failed to create workspace directory: {}",
209:                 workspace_path
210:             ))?;
211:         }
212: 
213:         if !workspace.is_dir() {
214:             anyhow::bail!("Workspace path is not a directory: {}", workspace_path);
215:         }
216: 
217:         
218:         let metadata = detect_project_metadata(workspace)?;
219: 
220:         
221:         let project = ProjectRecord {
222:             project_id: format!("proj-{}", Utc::now().timestamp_millis()),
223:             name,
224:             description,
225:             workspace_path,
226:             created_at: Utc::now(),
227:             last_opened_at: None,
228:             status: ProjectStatus::Active,
229:             metadata,
230:         };
231: 
232:         
233:         self.registry.projects.push(project.clone());
234:         self.save_registry_to_file()?;
235: 
236:         Ok(project)
237:     }
238: 
239:     
240:     pub fn get_all_projects(&self, options: Option<ProjectQueryOptions>) -> Vec<ProjectRecord> {
241:         let mut projects = self.registry.projects.clone();
242: 
243:         
244:         if let Some(opts) = options {
245:             if let Some(status) = opts.status {
246:                 projects.retain(|p| p.status == status);
247:             }
248: 
249:             if let Some(search) = opts.search {
250:                 let search_lower = search.to_lowercase();
251:                 projects.retain(|p| {
252:                     p.name.to_lowercase().contains(&search_lower)
253:                         || p.description
254:                             .as_ref()
255:                             .map(|d| d.to_lowercase().contains(&search_lower))
256:                             .unwrap_or(false)
257:                         || p.workspace_path.to_lowercase().contains(&search_lower)
258:                 });
259:             }
260: 
261:             if let Some(limit) = opts.limit {
262:                 projects.truncate(limit);
263:             }
264:         }
265: 
266:         
267:         projects.sort_by(|a, b| match (&a.last_opened_at, &b.last_opened_at) {
268:             (Some(at_a), Some(at_b)) => at_b.cmp(at_a),
269:             (Some(_), None) => std::cmp::Ordering::Less,
270:             (None, Some(_)) => std::cmp::Ordering::Greater,
271:             (None, None) => b.created_at.cmp(&a.created_at),
272:         });
273: 
274:         projects
275:     }
276: 
277:     
278:     pub fn get_project(&self, project_id: &str) -> Option<ProjectRecord> {
279:         self.registry
280:             .projects
281:             .iter()
282:             .find(|p| p.project_id == project_id)
283:             .cloned()
284:     }
285: 
286:     
287:     pub fn delete_project(&mut self, project_id: &str) -> Result<()> {
288:         let index = self
289:             .registry
290:             .projects
291:             .iter()
292:             .position(|p| p.project_id == project_id)
293:             .ok_or_else(|| anyhow::anyhow!("Project not found: {}", project_id))?;
294: 
295:         self.registry.projects.remove(index);
296:         self.save_registry_to_file()?;
297: 
298:         Ok(())
299:     }
300: 
301:     
302:     pub fn update_project(
303:         &mut self,
304:         project_id: &str,
305:         name: Option<String>,
306:         description: Option<String>,
307:         status: Option<ProjectStatus>,
308:     ) -> Result<ProjectRecord> {
309:         let index = self
310:             .registry
311:             .projects
312:             .iter()
313:             .position(|p| p.project_id == project_id)
314:             .ok_or_else(|| anyhow::anyhow!("Project not found: {}", project_id))?;
315: 
316:         let project = &mut self.registry.projects[index];
317: 
318:         if let Some(name) = name {
319:             project.name = name;
320:         }
321: 
322:         if let Some(description) = description {
323:             project.description = Some(description);
324:         }
325: 
326:         if let Some(status) = status {
327:             project.status = status;
328:         }
329: 
330:         
331:         let result = project.clone();
332: 
333:         self.save_registry_to_file()?;
334: 
335:         Ok(result)
336:     }
337: 
338:     
339:     pub fn update_last_opened(&mut self, project_id: &str) -> Result<()> {
340:         if let Some(project) = self
341:             .registry
342:             .projects
343:             .iter_mut()
344:             .find(|p| p.project_id == project_id)
345:         {
346:             project.last_opened_at = Some(Utc::now());
347:             self.save_registry_to_file()?;
348:         }
349: 
350:         Ok(())
351:     }
352: 
353:     
354:     pub fn auto_register_current_project(&mut self) -> Result<Option<ProjectRecord>> {
355:         let current_dir = std::env::current_dir().context("Failed to get current directory")?;
356: 
357:         
358:         if !is_cowork_project(&current_dir)? {
359:             return Ok(None);
360:         }
361: 
362:         let workspace_path = current_dir.to_string_lossy().to_string();
363: 
364:         
365:         if self
366:             .registry
367:             .projects
368:             .iter()
369:             .any(|p| p.workspace_path == workspace_path)
370:         {
371:             return Ok(None);
372:         }
373: 
374:         
375:         let project_name = current_dir
376:             .file_name()
377:             .and_then(|n| n.to_str())
378:             .unwrap_or("Untitled Project")
379:             .to_string();
380: 
381:         
382:         let project = self.register_project(workspace_path, project_name, None)?;
383: 
384:         Ok(Some(project))
385:     }
386: }
387: ⋮----
388: get_registry_path
389: ⋮----
390: ()
391: ⋮----
392: is_cowork_project
393: ⋮----
394: (path: &Path)
395: ⋮----
396: detect_project_metadata
397: ⋮----
398: (workspace: &Path)
399: ⋮----
400: detect_project_type
401: ⋮----
402: (workspace: &Path)
403: ⋮----
404: detect_technology_stack
405: ⋮----
406: (workspace: &Path)
407: ⋮----
408: test_registry_default
409: ⋮----
410: ()
411: ⋮----
412: test_project_metadata_default
413: ⋮----
414: ()
```

### litho.docs/en/3.Workflow.md (550 lines)

````
1: Based on the comprehensive research materials provided, I have analyzed the Cowork Forge system architecture, domain modules, and implementation details. Here is the complete Core Workflows documentation:
2: 
3: ---
4: 
5: # Core Workflows
6: 
7: **Document Version**: 1.0  
8: **System**: Cowork Forge - AI-Powered Iterative Software Development Platform  
9: **Generation Time**: 2026-02-14 05:11:46 (UTC)
10: 
11: ## 1. Workflow Overview
12: 
13: ### 1.1 System Architecture Context
14: 
15: Cowork Forge implements a **domain-driven, event-driven architecture** centered around a 7-stage AI agent pipeline. The system operates as a multi-crate Rust workspace with three primary distribution channels: a CLI tool for automation-focused users, a Tauri-based desktop GUI for interactive development, and a core domain library containing the business logic.
16: 
17: ### 1.2 Core Execution Paradigm
18: 
19: The system follows an **Actor-Critic Pattern with Human-in-the-Loop (HITL) Gates**:
20: 
21: ```mermaid
22: flowchart TB
23:     subgraph Pipeline["7-Stage Pipeline"]
24:         direction TB
25:         S1[Idea] --> S2[PRD] --> S3[Design] --> S4[Plan] --> S5[Coding] --> S6[Check] --> S7[Delivery]
26:     end
27:     
28:     subgraph Pattern["Actor-Critic Pattern per Stage"]
29:         Actor[AI Actor<br/>Generates Artifacts] --> Artifact[Stage Artifacts]
30:         Critic[AI Critic<br/>Validates Quality] -->|Feedback| Actor
31:         Artifact --> HITL{HITL Gate}
32:         HITL -->|Pass| NextStage
33:         HITL -->|Feedback| Actor
34:         HITL -->|Edit| ExternalEditor
35:     end
36:     
37:     Pipeline --> Pattern
38: ```
39: 
40: ### 1.3 Key Process Nodes
41: 
42: | Node | Responsibility | Criticality |
43: |------|---------------|-------------|
44: | **Pipeline Controller** | Orchestrates stage execution, manages context state, handles transitions | Critical |
45: | **Stage Executor** | Bridges pipeline with ADK framework, manages agent lifecycle and streaming | Critical |
46: | **Interactive Backend** | Abstraction for HITL interactions (CLI vs GUI implementations) | High |
47: | **Memory Store** | Persistence layer for project knowledge and iteration history | High |
48: | **Tool Registry** | 30+ ADK tools for file operations, validation, and data management | Medium |
49: | **Rate-Limited LLM** | Decorator ensuring API compliance (30 req/min, concurrency=1) | Medium |
50: 
51: ### 1.4 Process Coordination Mechanisms
52: 
53: - **Async/Await Concurrency**: Tokio runtime manages concurrent pipeline execution and I/O
54: - **Event-Driven GUI**: Tauri AppHandle emits real-time events (agent_event, tool_call, progress)
55: - **State Isolation**: Each iteration maintains isolated workspace under `.cowork-v2/iterations/{id}/`
56: - **Memory Aggregation**: Three-scope query system (Project-level, Iteration-level, Smart Merged)
57: 
58: ## 2. Main Workflows
59: 
60: ### 2.1 Genesis Iteration Creation Flow
61: 
62: **Business Value**: Transforms natural language ideas into complete software projects through structured AI-driven development, establishing baseline project memory for future evolution.
63: 
64: **Execution Path**:
65: 
66: ```mermaid
67: sequenceDiagram
68:     participant User
69:     participant CLI as CLI/GUI Interface
70:     participant PC as Pipeline Controller
71:     participant SE as Stage Executor
72:     participant ADK as ADK Framework
73:     participant LLM as LLM Provider
74:     participant Store as Iteration Store
75:     
76:     User->>CLI: cowork iter --project <name> <idea>
77:     CLI->>PC: Initialize PipelineContext
78:     PC->>Store: Create genesis iteration (Draft state)
79:     
80:     loop 7 Stages (Idea→Delivery)
81:         PC->>SE: Execute stage with context
82:         SE->>ADK: Create agent with instructions
83:         ADK->>LLM: Streaming inference requests
84:         LLM-->>ADK: Token stream
85:         ADK-->>SE: Tool execution requests
86:         SE-->>ADK: Tool results
87:         
88:         alt HITL Required
89:             SE->>CLI: Request confirmation
90:             CLI-->>User: Display artifact/preview
91:             User-->>CLI: Pass/Edit/Feedback
92:             CLI-->>SE: Response
93:             opt Feedback provided
94:                 SE->>SE: Execute with feedback (regeneration)
95:             end
96:         end
97:         
98:         SE->>Store: Persist stage artifacts
99:         PC->>PC: Transition to next stage
100:     end
101:     
102:     PC->>Store: Update iteration status (Completed)
103:     PC->>Memory: Generate knowledge snapshot
104: ```
105: 
106: **Key Technical Details**:
107: 
108: 1. **Stage Initialization**: Each stage implements the `Stage` trait with `execute()` and `execute_with_feedback()` methods
109: 2. **Artifact Persistence**: Stage outputs saved as markdown files (idea.md, prd.md, design.md, plan.md) in iteration workspace
110: 3. **Actor-Critic Implementation**: 
111:    - Actor instructions (~200 lines each) define workflow steps and tool schemas
112:    - Critic instructions enforce quality gates (anti-loop protection, simplicity validation)
113: 4. **Confirmation Gates**: Critical stages (Idea, PRD, Design, Plan, Coding) return `needs_confirmation() = true`
114: 
115: ### 2.2 Evolution Iteration Flow
116: 
117: **Business Value**: Enables incremental development and refactoring by leveraging historical context while supporting three inheritance strategies.
118: 
119: **Change Scope Analysis Algorithm**:
120: 
121: ```mermaid
122: flowchart TD
123:     A[User Input: Change Description] --> B{Keyword Analysis}
124:     
125:     B -->|architecture, refactor, redesign| C[Full Inheritance<br/>Artifacts + Code]
126:     B -->|feature, add, implement| D[Partial Inheritance<br/>Code Only]
127:     B -->|fix, bug, tweak| E[No Inheritance<br/>Fresh Start]
128:     
129:     C --> F{Stage Selection}
130:     D --> F
131:     E --> F
132:     
133:     F -->|fundamental change| G[Start: Idea Stage]
134:     F -->|requirements change| H[Start: PRD Stage]
135:     F -->|architecture change| I[Start: Design Stage]
136:     F -->|implementation change| J[Start: Plan Stage]
137:     
138:     G --> K[Load Base Knowledge]
139:     H --> K
140:     I --> K
141:     J --> K
142:     
143:     K --> L[Resume Pipeline]
144:     L --> M[Execute Remaining Stages]
145: ```
146: 
147: **Inheritance Mode Matrix**:
148: 
149: | Mode | Artifact Transfer | Code Transfer | Use Case |
150: |------|------------------|---------------|----------|
151: | **None** | ❌ | ❌ | Fresh start, experimental changes |
152: | **Partial** | ❌ | ✅ | Incremental features, bug fixes |
153: | **Full** | ✅ | ✅ | Major refactoring, architecture changes |
154: 
155: **Process Details**:
156: 1. **NLP Analysis**: `analyze_change_scope()` in `iteration.rs` uses keyword matching to determine optimal starting stage
157: 2. **Knowledge Loading**: `LoadBaseKnowledgeTool` retrieves historical decisions, patterns, and known issues from previous iterations
158: 3. **Workspace Preparation**: Inheritance modes determine which files are copied from base iteration workspace to new iteration workspace
159: 
160: ### 2.3 Human-in-the-Loop (HITL) Validation Flow
161: 
162: **Business Value**: Ensures human oversight at critical decision points while supporting iterative refinement through feedback loops.
163: 
164: ```mermaid
165: sequenceDiagram
166:     participant Agent as AI Agent
167:     participant Tool as HITL Tool
168:     participant Backend as InteractiveBackend
169:     participant User as Human User
170:     
171:     Agent->>Tool: Call review tool
172:     Tool->>Backend: Request interaction
173:     
174:     alt CLI Mode
175:         Backend->>User: Display preview (terminal)
176:         User->>Backend: Select option
177:         Note over User,Backend: 1. Pass (continue)<br/>2. Edit (open $EDITOR)<br/>3. Feedback (text input)
178:     else GUI Mode
179:         Backend->>User: Emit input_request event
180:         User->>Backend: Invoke response via Tauri
181:         Note over User,Backend: React modal with<br/>Pass/Edit/Feedback options
182:     end
183:     
184:     Backend-->>Tool: Return user action
185:     
186:     opt Edit Selected
187:         Tool->>Tool: Open external editor
188:         Tool->>Tool: Detect changes via hash comparison
189:     end
190:     
191:     Tool-->>Agent: Return result
192:     
193:     alt Feedback Provided
194:         Agent->>Agent: Regenerate with feedback context
195:         Agent->>Tool: Request review again
196:     end
197: ```
198: 
199: **Implementation Mechanics**:
200: - **CLI Backend**: Uses `dialoguer` crate for interactive prompts, supports opening system default editor via `edit` crate
201: - **GUI Backend**: Implements async request/response pattern using `tokio::sync::oneshot` channels with 3000s timeout
202: - **Content Review**: `ReviewAndEditContentTool` handles markdown content (15-line preview limit)
203: - **File Review**: `ReviewAndEditFileTool` handles file modifications (10-line preview, external editor integration)
204: 
205: ### 2.4 GUI Real-Time Execution & Monitoring Flow
206: 
207: **Business Value**: Provides transparent visibility into AI agent execution with real-time log streaming and development server management.
208: 
209: ```mermaid
210: flowchart TB
211:     subgraph Frontend["React Frontend"]
212:         Iterations[Iterations Panel] --> Invoke[Invoke Tauri Command]
213:         App[App Component] --> Listeners[Setup Event Listeners]
214:         Listeners --> Display[Real-time Display]
215:     end
216:     
217:     subgraph Backend["Tauri Backend"]
218:         Command[Command Handler] --> Async[Spawn Async Task]
219:         Async --> Executor[Iteration Executor]
220:         Executor --> Events[Emit Events]
221:         ProcessRunner[Process Runner] --> Stream[Stream Logs]
222:     end
223:     
224:     subgraph Core["Cowork Core"]
225:         Pipeline[Pipeline] --> Agent[Agent Orchestration]
226:         Agent --> Tools[Tool Execution]
227:         Agent --> LLM[LLM Integration]
228:     end
229:     
230:     Invoke --> Command
231:     Executor --> Pipeline
232:     Tools --> Events
233:     LLM --> Events
234:     Stream --> Events
235:     Events --> Display
236:     
237:     style Frontend fill:#f3e5f5
238:     style Backend fill:#e8f5e9
